Improve handoff semantics. The current HANDOFF.md template has Current State, What Was Done, Current Issues/Blockers, Next Steps, Important Context, and Progress Log sections, while aegis log currently appends progress entries. Add closeout validation that HANDOFF.md contains semantic current state and next steps that reflect the completed task rather than only appended logs. Implement a conservative parser/checker that requires non-placeholder content under Current State and Next Steps, requires the changed source evidence and strict verification evidence to be mentioned outside Progress Log, and rejects kickoff-only default text such as 'Task <id> has been kicked off through Aegis' as the final current state. Consider adding an aegis handoff-update or closeout --update-handoff option only if it can update Aegis-owned handoff sections deterministically; otherwise fail with actionable remediation telling the agent to use aegis log plus a handoff update before closeout.

Update Claude instructions and hooks so completion cannot remain advisory. Extend the installed Claude runtime entrypoint rendered by _render_claude_entrypoint in scripts/_aegis_installer.py, the packaged assets, and docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md to define the normal loop as readiness -> scope log -> scoped mutation -> pending tracking -> implementation log -> verification log -> strict verify -> closeout. Add .claude/commands/closeout.md and package it into installed targets if command files are managed for Claude. Update .claude/settings.json allowlist rendering to permit aegis closeout and ./.aegis/bin/aegis closeout. If a Stop hook can mechanically nudge or block final-task-complete phrasing, add a narrow closeout gate script or extend gate_lib.py with a Stop-time check that blocks when the agent attempts to end while plan-step-verify is complete but closeout has not passed; avoid blocking ordinary pauses that still have legitimate next steps. Any hook changes must be mirrored in root .claude/scripts and aegis_foundation/assets/.claude/scripts, and strict verification must validate the new hook/command registration where appropriate.

Produce a machine-readable report at .aegis/reports/closeout-report.json with schema_version, status, closed_at or checked_at, target_root, current_work task, readiness result, strict_verify report path/status, plan step statuses, evidence matrix by surface, handoff semantic checks, pending tracking summary, optional integration summary, git guidance, and failures. On success, consider updating current-work status to closed or closeout-passed while preserving enough state for strict verify and future inspection; if changing status from in-progress would break readiness or strict verify, record closeout_passed_at and closeout_report instead of changing the active status until a separate archive task exists.

Add optional Git/PR guidance without defaulting to gac. Align with templates/handlers/operators/git/create-commit-message.md and templates/shared/tools/tool-selection-matrix.md: recommend normal git add, git commit, git push, and gh pr create when the user delegates Git work and auth is available; mention gac only as an explicit user-requested legacy/manual convenience. Closeout should print or report suggested commands, not run commits or PR creation by default. Guard/docs should reject any new Aegis closeout guidance that makes gac the default.

Keep implementation portable. The closeout command must work in installed target projects that have no .taskmaster and no .serena directories. It may report those integrations as detected/absent and required/optional based on .aegis/state/current-work.json, following the same semantics already used by readiness and strict verification. Do not add mandatory Taskmaster set-status or Serena memory writes to the portable Aegis closeout path.
tasks.testStrategy:
Extend existing pytest suites instead of creating an unrelated harness. Add parser and CLI wiring tests in tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py for aegis closeout and scripts/codex-task aegis closeout. Add core closeout tests that install Aegis into a tmp target, initialize git, run kickoff, verify readiness READY, and assert closeout fails before scope, implementation, verification, strict verify, handoff semantic, and pending-tracking requirements are satisfied. Each failure should assert a precise failed check ID and nonzero CLI exit.

Add a live-agent-like end-to-end regression using tests/fixtures/aegis-target-projects/web-started, modeled on test_installed_web_target_real_feature_change_updates_full_workflow in tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py. The test should copy the fixture, install Aegis with Claude enabled, run kickoff for an Add Cart Button task without Taskmaster or Serena, confirm readiness READY, log scope with plan-step-scope completed, mutate src/main.ts to add a visible Add to cart button, simulate PostToolUse tracking, prove a second mutation and Stop are blocked until aegis log clears the pending source evidence, log implementation with plan-step-implement completed, run a real source-inspection verification command, log verification with plan-step-verify completed, run ./.aegis/bin/aegis verify --strict, update HANDOFF.md with semantic Current State and Next Steps, then run ./.aegis/bin/aegis closeout and assert status passed. Validate that source and verification evidence tokens appear in sessions/current, TRACKER.md, IMPLEMENTATION.md, CHANGELOG.md, HANDOFF.md, and plans/current, and that .aegis/reports/closeout-report.json records the same matrix.

Add negative installed-target regressions: closeout fails when readiness is BLOCKED; closeout fails when .aegis/state/pending-tracking.json contains an event; closeout fails when plan-step-verify is completed before plan-step-implement; closeout fails when strict verify has not been run or currently fails; closeout fails when source evidence is missing from one required surface; closeout fails when HANDOFF.md only has kickoff/default state plus appended Progress Log entries; closeout still passes in a target with no .taskmaster and no .serena; closeout fails only when current-work marks Taskmaster or Serena required and the integration is absent or invalid.

Add Claude adapter/asset tests in tests/claude_adapter/test_adapter_contract_files.py or tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py to assert installed CLAUDE.md or command assets instruct agents to run aegis closeout before declaring completion, settings allow aegis closeout and ./.aegis/bin/aegis closeout, and hook registration remains intact. Add guard tests where appropriate to ensure commit/PR guidance says normal git commands are the default and does not introduce default gac wording.

Run targeted verification commands before closing this task: python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/claude_adapter; python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked; git diff --check. If MCP closeout support is added, also run the focused MCP server/schema tests. Capture command evidence under the Task 117 reports folder and ensure the final closeout report path is referenced from session, TRACKER, IMPLEMENTATION, CHANGELOG, HANDOFF, and plans/current.

tasks.title: Native Global MCP Bootstrap for Aegis
tasks.description: Make Aegis registerable from any fresh project through native Claude and Codex MCP client commands, with deterministic command generation, optional execution, verification, and documentation for package, pinned, GitHub, local wheel, and source checkout install modes.
tasks.details: Codebase context: Aegis is already packaged in `pyproject.toml` as `aegis-foundation` with console scripts `aegis` and `aegis-mcp-server`; package CLI parsing lives in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`; the repository-local wrapper mirrors Aegis commands in `scripts/codex-task`; deterministic runtime behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; MCP server startup and discovery surfaces live in `aegis_mcp/server.py` through `AegisMCPConfig`, `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, `RESOURCE_URIS`, `PROMPT_NAMES`, `--default-target-dir`, `--transport`, and `--describe-config`; release docs currently show manual `.mcp.json` / Codex config snippets in `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md` and `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, mirrored into `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`; release and MCP coverage already exists in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`, and `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`. Implement a native MCP registration layer as the default bootstrap path. Add a deterministic registration module, preferably a small package module such as `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py` or a shared `scripts/_aegis_mcp_registration.py` if keeping the current script-core pattern is more consistent, with typed client specs for `claude` and `codex`, install source modes for unpinned package (`aegis-foundation`), pinned package (`aegis-foundation==<version>`), GitHub URL or ref, local wheel path, and source checkout path, plus stable JSON output containing client, scope, server name `aegis`, package spec, generated argv, rendered command, target dir, transport, and safety notes. Generate native command argv, not shell fragments, for examples including `claude mcp add --scope user aegis -- uvx --from aegis-foundation aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`, `claude mcp add --scope project aegis -- uvx --from aegis-foundation aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`, and `codex mcp add aegis -- uvx --from aegis-foundation aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`. Preserve the existing MCP server command shape from `aegis_mcp/server.py`; only the client registration wrapper changes. Add package CLI subcommands in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, and matching repo-local support in `scripts/codex-task`, for generate, execute, and verify flows, for example `aegis mcp generate-registration`, `aegis mcp execute-registration`, and `aegis mcp verify-registration` or an equivalent argparse shape that matches existing hyphenated command style. Generate mode must be read-only and print JSON by default, with an optional command rendering suitable for docs. Execute mode must locate the requested native client with `shutil.which`, run `subprocess.run` with the exact argv list, capture stdout/stderr/returncode into JSON, and never use `shell=True`. Verify mode must use native client inspection commands for Claude and Codex, parse their stdout/stderr robustly enough to recognize the `aegis` server, the `uvx --from ... aegis-mcp-server` command, `--default-target-dir`, and `--transport stdio`, and return structured pass/fail checks; add an optional stdio probe that launches the generated MCP server command and verifies discovery of `aegis.*` tools using the existing MCP client patterns from release tests. Missing client binaries must fail with a clear JSON error such as `status: missing_client` and include the command that would have been run. Make direct config-file writes an explicit fallback only: add a flag such as `--write-config-fallback` or `--fallback-config-file` for legacy/manual flows, keep it off by default, and ensure docs state native client registration is preferred. Ensure generated commands work without cloning `/home/loucmane/codex`: default to package assets, keep source checkout mode explicit, and verify `aegis-mcp-server --describe-config` reports `asset_origin: package` unless a source path was requested. Update docs in both `docs/aegis/` and packaged assets for global Claude, project Claude, Codex, pinned package, GitHub URL/ref, local wheel, and source checkout flows; replace manual `.mcp.json` as the primary path with native `claude mcp add` / `codex mcp add`, while retaining manual config snippets only under a fallback section. Update the release verification matrix to include native client registration, verification parsing, missing-client behavior, and fresh-project bootstrap acceptance. Acceptance: from a fresh directory with no checkout, a user can run the generated native client registration command globally or per-project, the native client can discover the Aegis MCP server and `aegis.*` tools, and the registered tool path can install packaged runtime assets and then run `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.verify`, and `aegis.closeout` without requiring this repository to exist locally.
tasks.testStrategy: Add focused unit and integration coverage, likely in a new `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`, plus documentation assertions in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`. Test command generation for each client/scope/source mode: Claude global user scope, Claude project scope, Codex default registration, unpinned package, pinned package, GitHub URL/ref, local wheel, and source checkout. Assert generated argv exactly matches native client semantics and includes `uvx`, `--from`, the selected package or artifact spec, `aegis-mcp-server`, `--default-target-dir`, the requested target, `--transport`, and `stdio`; assert rendered examples include the required commands from the task request. Test execute mode by monkeypatching `shutil.which` and `subprocess.run`; assert subprocess receives the expected argv list, cwd, environment, text capture settings, no shell invocation, and that stdout/stderr/returncode are surfaced in structured JSON. Test missing native clients for both `claude` and `codex` by forcing `shutil.which` to return `None`; assert nonzero status, no subprocess call, actionable error text, and generated command fallback in the payload. Test verify-mode parsing with fixture outputs for successful Claude registration, successful Codex registration, missing `aegis`, wrong package spec, wrong transport, and malformed/native-client error output. Add a fresh-project acceptance test using a temporary directory and fake native client binaries on PATH that record `mcp add` invocations and return realistic `mcp list` or `mcp get` output; then run the generated MCP command or an optional stdio probe to confirm discovery of `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, including `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.install`, `aegis.verify`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, and `aegis.closeout` where available. Reuse existing local wheel smoke patterns from `test_aegis_release_distribution.py` for optional environment-gated tests that build a wheel and run `uvx --from <wheel> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio` outside the repo. Add docs tests asserting `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`, its packaged asset copy, `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, and `docs/aegis/release-verification-matrix.md` cover native Claude global, Claude project, Codex, pinned package, GitHub URL, local wheel, source checkout, fallback config-file write, and no-clone acceptance language. Run focused tests: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`. Final workflow gates should include `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`.

tasks.title: Prove Local-First Aegis Package and MCP Installation Before PyPI
tasks.description: Build and validate local wheel and sdist artifacts for `aegis-foundation`, then prove a fresh project can register and run the Aegis MCP workflow from only the local artifact or package-style local project path. This task makes local artifact installation the required gate before any TestPyPI or PyPI publishing work.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis summary: `pyproject.toml` already defines the Python 3.11 distribution `aegis-foundation` with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`; `MANIFEST.in` and `[tool.setuptools.package-data]` include `aegis_foundation/assets/**`. The deterministic installer/runtime source of truth is `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, and package invocation delegates through `aegis_foundation/cli.py`. MCP startup and tools live in `aegis_mcp/server.py` via `AegisMCPConfig`, `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, `RESOURCE_URIS`, and `PROMPT_NAMES`. Native MCP registration support lives in `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py` with package, pinned, GitHub, wheel, and source modes, surfaced through `aegis mcp generate-registration`, `execute-registration`, and `verify-registration`, with mirrored repo-local argparse support in `scripts/codex-task`. Existing tests already cover cross-project installs (`tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py`), invocation contracts (`test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`), release distribution metadata and env-gated local wheel smokes (`test_aegis_release_distribution.py`), native MCP registration (`test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`), and MCP target flows (`test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`). The remaining gap is a mandatory, fresh-folder, local-artifact proof that exercises registration plus the full workflow through closeout without depending on `/home/loucmane/codex`, `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT`, repository-local `scripts/codex-task`, `.taskmaster/`, or `.serena/`.

Implementation approach:
1. Create normal Task 119 work-tracking artifacts and reports under the current repository workflow before changing code. Record the local-first release decision: no TestPyPI/PyPI publishing task may start until the local artifact proof report passes.
2. Harden local artifact production. Use the existing `aegis certify-release` path in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` as the preferred release-candidate authority. Ensure it builds both wheel and sdist using `uv build --sdist --wheel --out-dir <dist>` when `uv` is available, falls back to `python -m build` only when appropriate, computes SHA-256 checksums, inspects wheel/sdist contents for required package assets, entry points, schemas, MCP server package, workflow templates, Claude gate scripts, and packaged docs, and writes durable evidence to a report such as `reports/aegis-local-install-proof/local-artifact-proof.json` or the existing release certification report path if that is the better local pattern.
3. Make local artifact and local package-path MCP registration first-class. In `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py`, verify that `--source-mode wheel --artifact <wheel>` and the local project/path mode generate `uvx --from <artifact-or-path> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio` without requiring `--source-root` or a repository checkout. Add path validation and structured errors for missing wheel, missing local project `pyproject.toml`, unsupported archive type, or artifact paths that resolve outside the caller-visible filesystem. Preserve argv-list execution with `subprocess.run(..., shell=False)` and keep manual config-file writes fallback-only.
4. Add a dedicated fresh-folder proof harness, likely `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_local_artifact_mcp_proof.py`. The harness should build a local wheel/sdist into a temporary dist directory, create a brand-new temporary git project, clear `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT` and source-checkout `PYTHONPATH` leakage, register Aegis MCP using the native registration command generated from the local wheel or package path, start the MCP server through the generated `mcp_server_argv` with `mcp.client.stdio`, and prove the server reports packaged assets via `aegis-mcp-server --describe-config` or equivalent structured config evidence.
5. Exercise the complete workflow from the fresh target through MCP and/or the installed artifact command only: call `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install` with explicit apply, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log` for scope evidence, make a small task-scoped source or docs change in the new git project, record S:W:H:E tracking with `aegis.log`, run `aegis.verify` including strict verification when closeout requires it, log verification evidence, then call `aegis.closeout` with the required acknowledgement/update-handoff semantics. Assert that `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-plan.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`, and `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json` exist and show passing status.
6. Prove no checkout dependence. After the fresh-folder proof, recursively scan target `.aegis/` state, reports, generated contract files, current session, current plan, and work-tracking surfaces to ensure they do not contain `/home/loucmane/codex` or any source checkout path unless the specific test is intentionally exercising local project-path mode and the path appears only in the native client registration command evidence, not in installed target runtime state.
7. Keep fake-client and live-client layers separate. Use deterministic fake `claude` and `codex` binaries in tests to prove native registration execution and verification without requiring real clients on CI. Add an optional live smoke, gated by explicit environment variables, that runs the same local wheel registration against real installed native clients when available; the local artifact proof itself should still be runnable on a developer machine without publishing anything.
8. Update docs and packaged doc mirrors in `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`, `docs/aegis/release-policy.md`, `docs/aegis/release-verification-matrix.md`, and `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/*`. Document exact pre-publication commands for local wheel/sdist build, local wheel MCP registration, local project-path MCP registration, fresh-folder install/kickoff/log/verify/closeout, expected report locations, and the rule that TestPyPI/PyPI publishing is blocked until this proof passes. Keep public package snippets present but clearly downstream of this local proof.
9. Update CI/release templates only as documentation unless the repository already has a concrete CI workflow for Aegis release gates. If adding a command or report flag, prefer extending `aegis certify-release` rather than creating a separate release engine.
10. Preserve existing compatibility: do not break `scripts/codex-task aegis ...`, existing package CLI commands, existing MCP tool names, generated `.aegis/` paths, existing env-gated wheel smoke tests, or Task 111/115/117 target fixtures. The task is complete only when a brand-new temporary project can use the built local artifact plus the generated native registration command to complete inspect, install, kickoff, S:W:H:E log, verify, and closeout without cloning or invoking this repository checkout.
tasks.testStrategy:
Required focused verification:
- Add or extend pytest coverage for local artifacts and fresh-folder proof, preferably `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_local_artifact_mcp_proof.py`.
- Build artifacts during the proof with `uv build --sdist --wheel --out-dir <tmp-dist>` or the existing `aegis certify-release` build path. Assert exactly one `aegis_foundation-*.whl` and one `aegis_foundation-*.tar.gz`, valid SHA-256 values, expected console entry points, and required packaged assets including `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/codex-task`, `aegis_foundation/assets/schemas/aegis/*.json`, `aegis_foundation/assets/.claude/scripts/*`, `aegis_foundation/assets/templates/aegis/workflow/*`, and packaged `docs/aegis/*`.
- Unit-test native registration for local artifact and local package-path modes in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`: generated argv, rendered command, JSON payload, missing artifact failures, missing local project failures, no `shell=True`, and deterministic fake-client `execute-registration` / `verify-registration` behavior for both Claude and Codex.
- Add the fresh-folder MCP proof: create a temporary git project with no `.taskmaster`, no `.serena`, and no source-checkout `PYTHONPATH`; register Aegis from the built wheel or local package path; start `aegis-mcp-server` through the generated `uvx --from <artifact-or-path>` command; list tools/resources/prompts; call `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.verify`, and `aegis.closeout`; assert all required reports pass and the S:W:H:E token appears in session, tracker, implementation, changelog, handoff, and plan evidence where required by closeout.
- Assert negative cases: registration fails clearly for a missing artifact; wheel/source mode refuses absent `--artifact`; verify-registration fails for wrong package spec or transport; closeout fails before required log/verify evidence; target state does not contain `/home/loucmane/codex` or accidental checkout paths; public publishing docs are not marked ready without the local proof report.
- Keep existing targeted suites green: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py`.
- Run a required local pre-publication command sequence and capture evidence in the Task 119 reports folder: build local wheel/sdist, run the fresh-folder proof, run `aegis certify-release` without skipping artifact inspection, and verify the proof report records artifact paths, checksums, native registration command, MCP discovery, install report, kickoff report, S:W:H:E log evidence, verification report, closeout report, and no-checkout-dependence scan.
- Final repository gates: `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`.

tasks.title: Prove Fresh-Project Aegis Local Artifact Install and Claude Live-Test Rehearsal
tasks.description: Run a real-user-style pre-publication proof that Aegis can be built from this repository, registered from a local wheel or local package path, installed into a brand-new project outside the repo, and used through the Aegis workflow without PyPI/TestPyPI. Capture durable evidence and prepare a Claude live-test prompt that drives a small app change through install, kickoff, logging, verification, and closeout.
tasks.details: Codebase context: `pyproject.toml` defines the `aegis-foundation` Python package with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`, with packaged runtime assets under `aegis_foundation/assets/**`. `aegis_foundation/cli.py` delegates package-style commands to `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, including `inspect`, `plan-install`, `install`, `status`, `verify`, `closeout`, `certify-release`, `kickoff`, `log`, and the `mcp generate-registration`, `mcp execute-registration`, and `mcp verify-registration` subcommands. `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py` is the native registration helper and already supports package, pinned, GitHub, local wheel, and local source modes that render `uvx --from <artifact-or-source> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`. `aegis_mcp/server.py` exposes the MCP surface through `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install`, `aegis.verify`, `aegis.closeout`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.list_profiles`, and `aegis.explain_profile`, plus resources such as `aegis://manifest/current`, `aegis://work/current`, `aegis://verification/latest`, and `aegis://closeout/latest`. Existing regression coverage lives in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py`, and `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py`; this task should use those patterns but capture a fresh, human-readable Task 120 proof rather than relying only on prior automated smoke results. Implementation approach: 1. Kick off Task 120 with normal session, plan, and work-tracking surfaces. Create a Task 120 evidence area under the active work-tracking reports folder, for example `docs/ai/work-tracking/active/<task120-folder>/reports/fresh-local-artifact-install/`, and record the no-publishing boundary: no TestPyPI, no PyPI, no `twine upload`, no registry install of `aegis-foundation`; only local wheel or local source path may be used. 2. Build local artifacts from this repository using the existing release path where practical: `aegis certify-release --source-dir . --dist-dir <task120-report-dir>/dist --report-file <task120-report-dir>/certification-report.json`, or `uv build --sdist --wheel --out-dir <task120-report-dir>/dist` followed by the existing artifact inspection checks. Capture wheel and sdist paths, SHA-256 values, package version, Python version, git branch/commit, and required package members: console entry points, `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, `aegis_mcp/server.py`, `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/codex-task`, `aegis_foundation/assets/.claude/scripts/*`, `aegis_foundation/assets/schemas/aegis/*.json`, `aegis_foundation/assets/templates/aegis/workflow/*`, and packaged `docs/aegis/*`. 3. Create a genuinely fresh target project outside `/home/loucmane/codex`, preferably under `/tmp/aegis-task120-fresh-project` or another clearly external path. Seed it as a small app a real user might have, not a copy of this repository: for example a minimal web app with `package.json`, `index.html`, and `src/main.ts`, or a minimal Python app with `pyproject.toml`, source, and one test. Initialize a git repository on `main`. Assert there is no `.aegis`, `.claude`, `.taskmaster`, `.serena`, `sessions`, `plans`, or `docs/ai/work-tracking` before install. 4. Configure or register Aegis from the local artifact exactly as a real user would. Use the package CLI or local wheel invocation to generate the native Claude command: `aegis mcp generate-registration --client claude --scope project --source-mode wheel --artifact <absolute-wheel-path> --target-dir <fresh-target>`. Capture the JSON payload, including `client_argv`, `mcp_server_argv`, `rendered_command`, `package_spec`, `source_mode`, environment, and safety notes. If the real `claude` CLI is available, execute `aegis mcp execute-registration --client claude --scope project --source-mode wheel --artifact <absolute-wheel-path> --target-dir <fresh-target> --cwd <fresh-target>` and then `aegis mcp verify-registration` with the same inputs. If the real client is unavailable, record the structured `missing_client` result as a finding and still run the generated MCP server command directly through stdio as proof of the artifact path; do not silently substitute manual `.mcp.json` edits as the happy path. 5. Verify installed-artifact MCP startup from the fresh target with no checkout leakage. Clear `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT`, avoid source-checkout `PYTHONPATH`, and run `uvx --from <absolute-wheel-path> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir <fresh-target> --describe-config`. The output must report `distribution_name: aegis-foundation`, `asset_origin: package`, and a packaged asset root, not `/home/loucmane/codex`. Then start stdio using the generated `mcp_server_argv` and verify tool, resource, and prompt discovery against the expected names from `aegis_mcp.server`. 6. Install Aegis into the fresh target through the local artifact path, using the MCP tools where possible and the installed `aegis` command or `.aegis/bin/aegis` only where that mirrors a real user fallback. Run `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install` with explicit apply semantics, and `aegis.verify` with report-write acknowledgement as appropriate. Confirm `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/contract.md`, `.aegis/reports/install-plan.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/scripts/readiness.sh`, `.claude/scripts/pretooluse-gate.sh`, `.claude/scripts/posttooluse-tracking.sh`, `.claude/scripts/tracking-stop-gate.sh`, `.claude/scripts/gate_lib.py`, and `.aegis/bin/aegis` exist. 7. Prove readiness and kickoff behavior. Before kickoff, `bash .claude/scripts/readiness.sh --quick --root <fresh-target>` should block because there is no task-scoped current work; representative PreToolUse mutations should also block. Run `aegis.kickoff` or `.aegis/bin/aegis kickoff --target-dir . --task 1 --slug local-artifact-live-proof --title Local Artifact Live Proof --goal <goal>` from the fresh target. Confirm the kickoff creates `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, and an active `docs/ai/work-tracking/active/<date>-task1-local-artifact-live-proof-ACTIVE/` folder with `TRACKER.md`, `FINDINGS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `reports/`, and `designs/`. After kickoff, readiness must print a READY result for task 1 and must not require `.taskmaster` or `.serena`. 8. Prove tracking gates in the installed fresh project. Run a representative allowed source edit through the Claude hook scripts: PreToolUse allows the first task-scoped mutation after readiness; PostToolUse records `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json`; a second mutation and Stop gate are blocked until `aegis log` records the pending evidence; `aegis log --handler <handler> --evidence <path> --note <past-tense note> --plan-step plan-step-implement --plan-status completed` clears the pending event and writes S:W:H:E tokens into the current session, active tracker, implementation log, changelog, handoff, and plan. Also verify protected Aegis-owned paths such as `CODEX.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, and hook scripts are blocked from direct edits by installed gates. 9. Run strict verification and closeout from the fresh target. Use `.aegis/bin/aegis verify --target-dir . --strict`, log verification evidence with `aegis log`, then run `.aegis/bin/aegis closeout --target-dir . --update-handoff`. Capture the verification and closeout JSON reports and ensure required checks pass or, if they do not, record the exact gap in `FINDINGS.md` and the Task 120 report before any publishing task proceeds. 10. Add a source-checkout leakage scan over the fresh target and Task 120 evidence. Search target runtime state, reports, generated contracts, session, plan, and work-tracking files for `/home/loucmane/codex`, `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT`, source-checkout `PYTHONPATH`, and registry install commands. The only acceptable source-repo path references are Task 120 evidence metadata in this repository; installed target runtime should rely on the local wheel or packaged assets only. 11. Prepare a Claude live-test prompt as a committed evidence artifact, for example `<task120-report-dir>/claude-live-test-prompt.md`. The prompt should instruct Claude, when opened in the fresh target with the local-wheel Aegis MCP registered, to use discovered Aegis tools or `.aegis/bin/aegis` to inspect status, confirm readiness, perform one small app change such as adding a visible status/footer/button or a tiny health endpoint, record scope and implementation evidence through `aegis.log`, run an app-level verification command, run Aegis strict verify and closeout, and report only after the Aegis closeout passes. The prompt must explicitly say not to edit `.aegis/`, `.claude/`, `CLAUDE.md`, or `AGENTS.md` directly and to document any missing MCP tools or gate failures as findings. 12. Summarize findings in the active Task 120 `FINDINGS.md` and handoff. The final finding must state whether fresh-project local artifact installation is publishing-ready, publishing-blocked, or partially proven, and list any concrete gaps before TestPyPI/PyPI can begin.
tasks.testStrategy: Required verification: 1. Run the existing focused Aegis regression slice after any changes: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py`. Include the invocation-contract tests if documentation or command wording changes: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`. 2. Run the environment-gated local artifact smokes where the machine has `uv` and `uvx`: `AEGIS_RUN_WHEEL_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py::test_local_wheel_cli_smoke_when_enabled`; `AEGIS_RUN_WHEEL_MCP_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py::test_local_wheel_mcp_stdio_smoke_when_enabled`; `AEGIS_RUN_WHEEL_MCP_TARGET_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py::test_local_wheel_mcp_real_target_project_smoke_when_enabled`; and, if present in the current suite, the full release certification smoke such as `AEGIS_RUN_CERTIFICATION_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py::test_release_certification_full_clean_smoke_when_enabled`. 3. Verify the Task 120 manual fresh-project proof with captured command transcripts or JSON reports under work-tracking: local wheel/sdist build succeeds; checksums and required package members are recorded; native registration JSON uses `source_mode: wheel` or explicit local source mode; generated MCP command starts with `uvx --from <absolute-local-artifact-or-source-path>`; no command uses PyPI/TestPyPI, `twine upload`, `pip install aegis-foundation` from a registry, `test.pypi.org`, or `pypi.org` as part of the proof. 4. In the fresh target, assert the following states in order: initial project has none of the Aegis directories; `aegis-mcp-server --describe-config` from the wheel reports package assets; MCP stdio discovery includes the expected Aegis tools/resources/prompts; `aegis.inspect` reports not installed; `aegis.plan_install` is read-only; `aegis.install` refuses without apply and applies with apply; `aegis.verify` refuses without acknowledgement where required and passes when acknowledged; `aegis.status` reports current after install. 5. After kickoff, assert the generated files and scaffolding exist: `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `.aegis/bin/aegis`, `.aegis/reports/install-plan.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`, `.aegis/contract.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/scripts/readiness.sh`, `.claude/scripts/pretooluse-gate.sh`, `.claude/scripts/posttooluse-tracking.sh`, `.claude/scripts/tracking-stop-gate.sh`, `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, and the active work-tracking files `TRACKER.md`, `FINDINGS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and `HANDOFF.md`. 6. Exercise installed gates directly: readiness blocks before kickoff and returns READY after kickoff; PreToolUse blocks mutations while readiness is blocked; PreToolUse allows a valid task source edit after kickoff; PostToolUse creates pending tracking; a second mutation and Stop gate are blocked while pending tracking exists; `aegis log` clears pending tracking and updates S:W:H:E evidence in all required surfaces; protected Aegis-owned paths are refused; strict verify and closeout pass only after required evidence exists. 7. Scan the fresh target for source-checkout leakage: `rg -n '/home/loucmane/codex|AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT|PYTHONPATH=.*codex|test.pypi.org|pypi.org' <fresh-target>`. Treat any runtime-state hit as a blocker unless it is explicitly part of a captured command transcript stored in the Task 120 evidence folder outside the target. 8. Review the Claude live-test prompt artifact manually against the fresh target acceptance: it must tell Claude to use Aegis MCP or `.aegis/bin/aegis`, perform one small app change, log scope/implementation/verification evidence, run app verification, run `aegis verify --strict`, run `aegis closeout --update-handoff`, avoid direct Aegis-owned file edits, and document any gaps. 9. Run repository workflow gates before marking the task complete: `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`. 10. Acceptance is met only if the proof evidence shows no PyPI/TestPyPI usage, the fresh target was outside the repository, the local artifact/source path drove MCP registration or startup, installed Aegis assets and hooks were created, readiness and tracking gates behaved as designed, evidence was captured under Task 120 work-tracking, and `FINDINGS.md` clearly states any remaining gap before public publishing.

tasks.title: Improve Aegis Workflow UX and Logging Defaults
tasks.description: Harden Aegis installed workflow ergonomics after the Task 120 fresh local wheel and Claude live test by improving default log surfaces, closeout repair guidance, and pending-event consumption before TestPyPI/PyPI. Preserve the current architecture: Aegis MCP/CLI remains the workflow control plane, native tools remain the normal implementation path, and installed hooks enforce tracking and protected-path rules.
tasks.details: Codebase context from analysis: `pyproject.toml` packages `aegis-foundation` with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`; core workflow behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` and the packaged mirror `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; CLI wiring for `log`, `verify`, and `closeout` is in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`; MCP tool schemas and handlers are in `aegis_mcp/server.py`; installed hook behavior and pending tracking live in `.claude/scripts/gate_lib.py` and `aegis_foundation/assets/.claude/scripts/gate_lib.py`; current regression coverage is concentrated in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py`, `test_aegis_mcp_server.py`, `test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`, `test_aegis_release_distribution.py`, `test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`, and `test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`. Task 120 evidence in `docs/ai/work-tracking/archive/20260522-task120-fresh-local-install-proof-COMPLETED/reports/fresh-local-install-proof/claude-live-test-result.md` proved the intended install/kickoff/edit/log/verify/closeout path but exposed three UX gaps: agents had to know exact default surfaces, strict verify produced pending tracking that required exact handler/evidence copying, and closeout missing-evidence failures reported surfaces rather than actionable repair calls. Implementation approach: 1. Start Task 121 work-tracking normally and record Task 120 findings as scope evidence; keep PyPI/TestPyPI publication explicitly out of scope. 2. Replace the static `AEGIS_DEFAULT_LOG_SURFACES = ("implementation", "changelog", "handoff")` behavior in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` with event-class-aware default surface selection while keeping explicit `surfaces` overrides backward compatible. Add a small helper such as `_infer_log_event_class(plan_step, handler, evidence, explicit_event_class)` and `_default_log_surfaces_for_event(event_class, evidence)` so `log_work` can default correctly when `surfaces is None`. Scope logs should update the always-written session and tracker plus `FINDINGS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and the plan when `plan_step` is supplied. Implementation logs should update session, tracker, `IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and the plan when supplied. Verification logs should update session, tracker, `IMPLEMENTATION.md` or `FINDINGS.md` as appropriate, `CHANGELOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and the plan when supplied. Prefer explicit `event_class` support in CLI and MCP (`scope`, `implementation`, `verification`) and infer from `plan-step-scope`, `plan-step-implement`, `plan-step-verify`, handlers such as `aegis:verify`, and evidence such as `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json` only when omitted. 3. Extend `aegis_foundation/cli.py` and `aegis_mcp/server.py` so the CLI/MCP `aegis.log` surfaces expose the new defaults without breaking existing calls. Existing calls with repeated `--surface` or `surfaces: [...]` must keep exact behavior; omitted surfaces should use the new event-aware defaults. Update MCP schema tests that currently assert `surfaces.default is None` so they also assert the new `event_class` and pending-event input shape. 4. Improve pending-event ergonomics. Add support to `log_work` for consuming a pending event by id, for example CLI `aegis log --pending-id <id> --note ... --plan-step ... --plan-status ...` and MCP `aegis.log` input `pending_event_id`. If exactly one pending event exists, allow a documented `current` or `latest` sentinel only if the implementation can do so deterministically. When a pending id is provided, load `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json`, copy that event's `handler` and `evidence` into the S:W:H:E entry, validate the event belongs to current work, clear only the consumed event, and reject ambiguous or unknown ids with a response that lists valid ids. Keep normal native implementation tools untouched; this is a logging/control-plane ergonomics change, not an MCP editor. 5. Update pending-event guidance in `_format_pending_tracking_for_error` and installed hook messages in `gate_lib.py` so blocking output includes a copyable Aegis repair shape such as `aegis log --pending-id <id> --note "<past-tense note>" --plan-step plan-step-implement --plan-status completed` plus the explicit handler/evidence fallback. 6. Improve closeout repair guidance in `closeout()` without weakening gates. Add a deterministic `repair_guidance` or `repair_plan` section to `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json` whenever `closeout.evidence.*`, `closeout.plan.*`, `closeout.tracker.*`, or pending tracking gates fail. For missing evidence references, build a matrix from `evidence_matrix` and generate exact suggested `aegis log` calls when an existing S:W:H:E token for that evidence can be found on another surface and the handler/note can be inferred. If exact repair is not possible, emit a structured repair item with missing `surface`, `evidence`, suggested `event_class`, suggested `plan_step`, and placeholder handler/note fields rather than only reporting `details.missing`. Do not make closeout auto-log repairs; `--update-handoff` should remain limited to semantic handoff sections. 7. Keep source and packaged runtime in sync: mirror changes into `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py` and packaged hook/docs assets where applicable, and update installed docs/templates only where behavior changed, especially `docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md`, `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md`, generated Aegis instructions embedded in `_aegis_installer.py`, and any MCP docs/tests that describe `aegis.log`. 8. Add or adjust focused tests that reproduce the Task 120 live Claude path: install/kickoff into a representative web target, native edit creates pending tracking, one intended `aegis.log` call using pending-event consumption updates all required surfaces, strict verify passes, the strict verification log defaults update all closeout-required surfaces, and closeout passes without extra changelog-only relogging. 9. Record final Task 121 findings and verification evidence in active work-tracking, including whether these fixes remove the Task 120 publication blockers. Do not publish to TestPyPI or PyPI in this task.
tasks.testStrategy: Add focused unit coverage first: in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py`, cover event-aware default surface selection for scope, implementation, and verification logs; assert session/tracker are always updated, plan updates only occur when `plan_step` is supplied, scope defaults update findings/decisions/handoff, implementation defaults update implementation/changelog/handoff, and verification defaults update implementation-or-findings/changelog/handoff. Add regression coverage proving explicit `surfaces` still overrides defaults. Add pending-event tests that create `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json` through the installed hook helpers, call `log_work`/CLI with `pending_event_id` or `--pending-id`, assert handler/evidence are copied from the pending event, only the selected event is cleared, and ambiguous/unknown ids return actionable guidance. Add closeout repair guidance tests that intentionally omit evidence from one or more required surfaces and assert the closeout report contains exact suggested `aegis log` calls when possible and structured repair items otherwise. Update `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py` for the MCP schema and handler behavior, including backward compatibility for existing `aegis.log` calls and new `event_class`/`pending_event_id` inputs. Extend `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py` with a web target live-style workflow: install or use the Aegis shim, kickoff, edit `src/main.ts` through native-hook simulation, confirm pending tracking, consume the pending event with a single intended log call, run app-level verification, run strict verify, log verification with default surfaces, and assert closeout passes without additional relogging. Run the existing focused Aegis suite: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_contract_docs.py`. Where the environment supports it, also run the opt-in local wheel/MCP target smoke used by Task 120, especially `AEGIS_RUN_WHEEL_MCP_TARGET_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py::test_local_wheel_mcp_real_target_project_smoke_when_enabled`. Finish with repository gates: `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`. Acceptance requires focused tests for default surface selection, closeout repair guidance, pending-event consumption, and the no-extra-relogging web workflow, with existing installer/MCP/gate tests still passing and no TestPyPI/PyPI publication performed.

tasks.title: Advance Aegis Workflow Guidance and Adapter Portability
tasks.description: Add a next-level Aegis workflow guidance layer that tells agents exactly what to do next, makes plan-step logging deterministic, and documents release/adaptor portability without changing the core architecture. Aegis MCP/CLI remains the workflow control plane, native agent tools remain the implementation path, and installed hooks/gates remain the enforcement layer.
tasks.details: Codebase analysis: `pyproject.toml` defines the `aegis-foundation` package with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`. Core deterministic behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` and must be mirrored into `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; CLI wiring lives in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`; MCP tools/resources/prompts live in `aegis_mcp/server.py`; installed Claude guidance is rendered by `_render_claude_entrypoint()` in the installer; packaged docs live under `docs/aegis/` and `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`; live target fixtures already exist under `tests/fixtures/aegis-target-projects/{web-new,web-started,python-new,python-started,backend-new,backend-started}`. Task 121 already adds event-aware log defaults, `event_class`, `pending_event_id`, and closeout repair guidance; this task must explicitly build on that immediate-fix layer and keep longer-term release/adaptor work separated in a roadmap document. Implementation approach: 1. Add a read-only workflow guidance surface. Prefer an additive `aegis next` CLI command plus MCP tool `aegis.next`, and also embed the same `workflow_guidance` payload in existing `aegis.status` for backward-compatible discovery. Implement the core in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, for example `next_action(target_dir, *, source_root)`, so CLI and MCP share one deterministic path. The payload should include `phase`, `state`, `next_required_action`, `suggested_cli`, `suggested_mcp_call`, `missing_gates`, `copyable_repairs`, `read_only: true`, and `architecture_notes` that state Aegis controls workflow state while native agent tools perform implementation edits. It should cover at least these states: not installed -> inspect/plan-install/install; installed but no current work -> kickoff; current work without scope evidence -> log scope before editing; pending tracking exists -> log pending event; implementation evidence missing -> use native tools then log implementation; verification evidence missing -> run project verification then log verification; strict verification missing -> run `aegis verify --strict` then log its report; closeout gates incomplete -> run closeout-ready/dry-run repairs; closeout ready -> run strict closeout; closeout passed -> no workflow action required. 2. Add deterministic `plan_step=auto` support without introducing AI inference. Extend CLI `aegis log --plan-step auto`, MCP `aegis.log` input `plan_step: 'auto'`, and the shared `log_work()` path. Infer `plan-step-scope` only from explicit event class `scope`, a scope handler, or current guidance state that requires scope; infer `plan-step-implement` from a consumed pending mutation event or implementation event class; infer `plan-step-verify` from verification event class, verification handlers, app-level verification evidence, or `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`. Treat app-level verification and strict verification as subtypes under the existing `plan-step-verify` row: app verification may append evidence without necessarily completing the step, while strict verification report evidence may complete it. If more than one inference is possible, fail with an actionable error listing explicit valid `--plan-step` values instead of guessing from free-text notes. Return `plan.inferred`, `plan.inference_reason`, and `plan.strict_verification_evidence` metadata in the log payload. Preserve explicit plan-step behavior exactly. 3. Improve MCP tool descriptions in `aegis_mcp/server.py`. Update docstrings/descriptions for `aegis.status`, `aegis.next`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.verify`, and `aegis.closeout` so fresh Claude sessions naturally call status/next first, record scope before editing, use native tools for implementation, log pending mutation evidence, log verification before closeout, and never write `.aegis/` directly. Keep mutating MCP operations limited to Aegis workflow state and reports; do not add MCP source-editing tools. 4. Update installed Claude guidance generated by `_render_claude_entrypoint()` so the first visible operating loop is: run readiness/status/next, kickoff if needed, log scope with `--plan-step auto` before persistent edits, perform implementation with native Claude tools, consume pending tracking with `aegis log --pending-id current --plan-step auto`, run task verification, run strict verification, log strict verification with `--plan-step auto`, run closeout-ready/dry-run if blocked, then run strict closeout before claiming completion. Ensure `.claude/settings.json` allowlist guidance still permits `aegis status`, `aegis next`, `aegis log`, `aegis verify`, `aegis closeout`, and `./.aegis/bin/aegis ...` fallback calls. If adding a pre-mutation hook check for missing scope, implement it in `.claude/scripts/gate_lib.py` and packaged copies without deadlocking the `aegis log` repair command; otherwise document the scope-before-edit behavior as guidance and rely on closeout gates for enforcement. 5. Add a pre-closeout readiness surface. Either implement `aegis closeout --dry-run` or a separate `aegis closeout-ready` CLI/MCP read-only check; prefer sharing the same internal evaluator used by closeout so failure IDs and repair guidance match. The check should report missing plan steps, missing tracker checkboxes, pending tracking events, missing evidence references, missing strict verification evidence, and handoff semantic gaps before strict closeout. It must produce copyable repairs using Task 121 repair guidance, including `aegis log --pending-id <id> --plan-step auto`, explicit `aegis log --handler ... --evidence ... --surface ...` repairs when exact inference is possible, and structured placeholders when exact repair cannot be generated. It must not auto-log repairs or directly edit `.aegis/`. 6. Add MCP prompts for the requested workflow phases while preserving existing prompt names as aliases where useful. Add/update `PROMPT_NAMES` for `aegis.bootstrap`, `aegis.start_task`, `aegis.implement_task`, and `aegis.closeout_task`. `bootstrap` should drive inspect/status/plan-install/install/verify; `start_task` should drive status/next/kickoff/scope log; `implement_task` should state that native agent tools do implementation and Aegis logs workflow evidence; `closeout_task` should drive app verification, strict verification, verification logging, closeout-ready repairs, and closeout. Prompt text must say prompts are advisory and tool/report outputs are evidence. 7. Create a release-grade live acceptance matrix document, likely `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md`, and package it under `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`. The matrix must cover fresh and existing web, Python, and backend projects; explicit no-Taskmaster/no-Serena operation; a project that already has `.mcp.json`; native MCP registration; and CLI-shim fallback through `./.aegis/bin/aegis` when global `aegis` is unavailable. Each row should list starting project shape, command surface, expected next-action sequence, required reports, evidence paths, closeout criteria, accepted deferrals, and whether the row is automated, manual-live, or future release evidence. Update `docs/aegis/release-verification-matrix.md` to reference this matrix instead of duplicating it. 8. Add an adapter abstraction plan tied to Task 62. Create or update an adapter contract document, for example `docs/aegis/agent-adapter-contract.md`, and update `templates/registry/agent-compatibility-matrix.json` only where the schema already supports it. Document Claude as the default implemented adapter and Codex/Gemini/other agents as future/planned adapters. For each adapter, record entry files, hook capability, MCP registration path, mutation surfaces, managed/owned paths, protected paths, enforcement level (`mechanical`, `gated`, `policy-only`, or `planned`), evidence model, limitations, and required verification commands. This is a plan and contract task for non-Claude adapters, not a full Codex/Gemini runtime implementation. 9. Add TestPyPI/PyPI readiness only as documentation input. Update `docs/aegis/release-policy.md`, `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, and packaged mirrors with a checklist for metadata, versioning, artifact inspection, provenance, signing/checksums, dry-run validation, and publication decision records. Do not run `twine upload`, do not publish to TestPyPI/PyPI, and do not mark publication ready unless the live matrix and local artifact proofs are satisfied. 10. Maintain source/package parity. Any change to installer core, rendered guidance, docs, hook scripts, or workflow templates must be mirrored into packaged assets and validated by existing package-data/release tests. Update `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, `PROMPT_NAMES`, docs, and tests together so MCP discovery remains deterministic. 11. Produce a short roadmap artifact, preferably under the Task 122 active work-tracking `designs/` folder and referenced from `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md`, with three sections: Task 121 immediate fixes already handled or assumed; Task 122 workflow guidance/portability deliverables; deferred release publication and future adapter implementation work. Acceptance requires that roadmap to explicitly separate Task 121 repair work from longer-term release and adapter work.
tasks.testStrategy: Add focused unit, CLI, MCP, docs, and live-matrix coverage. In `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py`, cover `next_action`/`aegis next` states for not installed, installed without current work, missing scope log, pending tracking, implementation evidence required, verification evidence required, strict verification required, closeout-ready, closeout blocked, and closeout passed. Assert the guidance payload always includes a copyable CLI command and a structured MCP call, is read-only, and never recommends MCP source editing. Add `plan_step=auto` tests for scope, implementation pending-event consumption, app verification evidence, strict verification report evidence, explicit plan-step override, ambiguous inference failure, and returned inference metadata. Add closeout-ready/dry-run tests that intentionally omit plan/tracker/evidence/handoff requirements and assert missing gates plus copyable repairs are reported before strict closeout. In `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py`, update `V1_TOOL_NAMES` and `PROMPT_NAMES` expectations, assert `aegis.next` is read-only, assert `aegis.status` contains `workflow_guidance`, assert `aegis.log` schema accepts `plan_step: auto`, and inspect tool descriptions from `list_tools()` to verify they mention status/next, scope-before-edit, native implementation tools, pending logging, strict verification, closeout, and no direct `.aegis/` writes. Add prompt tests proving `aegis.bootstrap`, `aegis.start_task`, `aegis.implement_task`, and `aegis.closeout_task` contain the required phase guidance and evidence disclaimers. In `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`, extend the existing real-target fixture flow across `web-new`, `web-started`, `python-new`, `python-started`, `backend-new`, and `backend-started` to exercise status/next guidance, `plan_step=auto`, no-Taskmaster/no-Serena readiness, strict verification logging, and closeout. Add a fixture or generated target with an existing `.mcp.json` to prove native registration/fallback guidance does not clobber unrelated MCP config. Add a CLI-shim fallback test that removes global `aegis` from PATH and completes the guided log/verify/closeout sequence through `./.aegis/bin/aegis`. Add docs tests in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py` and/or `test_aegis_mcp_contract_docs.py` to assert packaged docs include `live-acceptance-matrix.md`, adapter contract coverage, TestPyPI/PyPI readiness as documentation-only, no publication command as an executed acceptance step, and release-verification matrix links to the live matrix. Add adapter-contract tests that validate Claude/Codex/Gemini/future adapter rows include entry files, hook capability, MCP registration, mutation surfaces, enforcement level, and verification commands. Run the focused Aegis suite: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_contract_docs.py`. Where the environment supports it, run the existing opt-in local wheel/MCP smoke and any new live-matrix smoke behind explicit environment variables, keeping TestPyPI/PyPI publication disabled. Finish with repository gates: `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`. Acceptance is met only when first-pass closeout guidance is testable, live matrix criteria are documented, MCP prompts/tool descriptions are covered by tests, `plan_step=auto` is deterministic, fresh Claude guidance leads to scope logging before edits and verification logging before closeout, and the roadmap separates Task 121 immediate fixes from release/adaptor follow-up work.

tasks.title: Prove Aegis Release Candidate Global MCP Install on a User-Supplied Existing Project
tasks.description: Build a local Aegis release-candidate artifact and prove it can be registered, started, installed, and used from outside this repository against a copied existing project supplied by the user. The proof must exercise the intended global MCP command path, use Aegis MCP for workflow control-plane operations, use native tools for source edits, and produce durable release evidence before any TestPyPI/PyPI decision.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis: `pyproject.toml` already packages `aegis-foundation` with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`, includes `aegis_foundation*`, `aegis_mcp*`, and packaged assets under `aegis_foundation/assets/**`. Package CLI wiring in `aegis_foundation/cli.py` delegates to `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` for `inspect`, `plan-install`, `install`, `status`, `next`, `kickoff`, `log`, `verify`, `closeout`, `certify-release`, and `mcp generate/execute/verify-registration`. Native global registration helpers in `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py` render `claude mcp add` and `codex mcp add` commands around `uvx --from <package-spec> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`, with package, pinned, GitHub, wheel, and source modes. MCP tool/resource/prompt discovery lives in `aegis_mcp/server.py`, where `V1_TOOL_NAMES` includes `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.next`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install`, `aegis.verify`, `aegis.closeout`, `aegis.closeout_ready`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.list_profiles`, and `aegis.explain_profile`. The installer already creates `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json`, `.aegis/bin/aegis`, sessions, plans, work-tracking scaffolds, hooks, reports, strict verification, and closeout reports through `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`. Existing validation lives in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`, `test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`, `test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`, `test_aegis_installer.py`, and docs under `docs/aegis/`, especially `distribution.md`, `mcp-client-setup.md`, `release-policy.md`, `release-verification-matrix.md`, and `live-acceptance-matrix.md`.

Implementation approach:
1. Treat this as the release-candidate live acceptance proof, not a packaging redesign. Reuse the existing `aegis certify-release` and `uv build --sdist --wheel` paths instead of inventing a parallel release engine. Preserve development checkout, editable, source-mode, and local-wheel paths already documented by Tasks 113, 114, 118, 119, 120, 121, and 122.
2. Create normal Task 123 workflow evidence in the repository before starting the proof. Use a report folder such as `docs/ai/work-tracking/active/<date>-task123-aegis-release-candidate-global-mcp-proof-ACTIVE/reports/release-candidate-global-mcp-proof/`. Record the user-supplied existing project path, the copied `/tmp` target path, date/time, git commit, package version, Python version, `uv`/`uvx` versions, native client versions when available, and the explicit no-publication boundary: no `twine upload`, no TestPyPI, no PyPI, and no registry install as an acceptance shortcut.
3. Build or expose the local distributable Aegis artifact from this repo. Prefer `uv build --sdist --wheel --out-dir /tmp/aegis-task123-dist` and/or `aegis certify-release --source-dir . --dist-dir /tmp/aegis-task123-dist --report-file <task123-report>/certification-report.json`. Preserve wheel and sdist paths, SHA-256 checksums, artifact member inspection, and certification output. Assert the artifact contains `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, `aegis_mcp/server.py`, `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/codex-task`, schemas, hooks, workflow templates, and packaged `docs/aegis/*`.
4. Copy the existing user-supplied project into `/tmp`, for example `/tmp/aegis-task123-existing-project-copy`, using a copy strategy that preserves normal project files while avoiding mutation of the original. Initialize or preserve git in the copy as appropriate, but the live test must only mutate the `/tmp` copy. Capture an initial file snapshot and `git status --short` from the copy. Confirm the copy is outside `/home/loucmane/codex` and record whether it already contains `.aegis`, `.claude`, `.mcp.json`, `.taskmaster`, `.serena`, `sessions`, `plans`, or `docs/ai/work-tracking`.
5. Register Aegis MCP from outside `/home/loucmane/codex` using the intended global/native command path. From the copied project directory, generate the registration payload with the package CLI or local artifact invocation, for example `aegis mcp generate-registration --client claude --scope user --source-mode wheel --artifact /tmp/aegis-task123-dist/aegis_foundation-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl --target-dir .` or the equivalent command run via the installed/local artifact `aegis`. Capture `client_argv`, `mcp_server_argv`, `rendered_command`, `package_spec`, `source_mode`, `environment`, and safety notes. If the real `claude` client is available, run `aegis mcp execute-registration --client claude --scope user --source-mode wheel --artifact <wheel> --target-dir . --cwd <copy>` and then `aegis mcp verify-registration` with the same inputs. If the real native client is unavailable, preserve the structured `missing_client` result and still start the generated MCP server command directly; do not make manual `.mcp.json` edits the happy path.
6. Prove `aegis-mcp-server` starts from the copied project and does not depend on the source checkout. Clear `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT` and avoid source-checkout `PYTHONPATH`. Run the generated `uvx --from <wheel> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --describe-config` from the copy and assert `distribution_name` is `aegis-foundation`, `asset_origin` is `package`, `default_target_dir` resolves to the copied project, and `source_root` points to packaged assets rather than `/home/loucmane/codex`. Then start stdio through the generated `mcp_server_argv` and verify tool, resource, and prompt discovery matches `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, `RESOURCE_URIS`, and `PROMPT_NAMES`.
7. Install Aegis into the copied existing project through MCP, not by directly editing `.aegis/`. Use `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.next`, `aegis.plan_install`, and `aegis.install` with `apply=true`. Preserve the read-only outputs for inspect/status/next/plan, prove `aegis.install` refuses without `apply=true`, then capture the applied install report. Confirm installation creates or updates `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/contract.md`, `.aegis/bin/aegis`, `.aegis/reports/install-plan.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/scripts/readiness.sh`, `.claude/scripts/pretooluse-gate.sh`, `.claude/scripts/posttooluse-tracking.sh`, `.claude/scripts/tracking-stop-gate.sh`, `.claude/scripts/gate_lib.py`, sessions/plans roots, and packaged workflow templates. For existing files, capture manual-review/conflict behavior and confirm unrelated project files are not overwritten.
8. Prove readiness and kickoff behavior in the existing-project copy. Before kickoff, run installed readiness (`bash .claude/scripts/readiness.sh --quick --root .`) and representative PreToolUse checks to show work is not ready or task-scoped yet. Then use MCP `aegis.kickoff` with `apply=true` for a task such as `task=123`, `slug=existing-project-global-mcp-proof`, and a clear title/goals. Confirm kickoff creates `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `.aegis/reports/kickoff-report.json`, `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, and an active `docs/ai/work-tracking/active/<date>-task123-existing-project-global-mcp-proof-ACTIVE/` folder with `TRACKER.md`, `FINDINGS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `reports/`, and `designs/`. After kickoff, readiness must return `READY` for task 123 and must not require Taskmaster or Serena unless the copied project explicitly requires them.
9. Run a realistic Claude-style workflow in the copy. Use Aegis MCP for workflow control-plane operations: `aegis.next`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.verify`, `aegis.closeout_ready`, and `aegis.closeout`. Use native tools for project implementation work: read files, edit source, run project tests or smoke commands, and inspect git diff/status. Start with a scope log through MCP `aegis.log` using `apply=true`, `event_class=scope` or `plan_step=auto`, and evidence pointing at the active `FINDINGS.md` or equivalent scope note.
10. Make one small, reversible, realistic source change in the copied project with native tools, chosen according to the copied project type. Examples: add a small CLI option, adjust a README-visible command output, add a tiny route/health response, add a visible UI button, or add/extend a small test-backed function. Do not edit `.aegis/`, `.claude/`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, or generated workflow files directly except through Aegis MCP/CLI. Use installed hook scripts to simulate Claude mutation tracking where possible: PreToolUse should allow the first task-scoped source edit, PostToolUse should record `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json`, a second mutation and Stop gate should block while pending tracking exists, and MCP `aegis.log` with `pending_event_id=current` and `plan_step=auto` should clear the pending event and write S:W:H:E evidence.
11. Run project-specific verification in the copied project with native tools. Choose a command appropriate to the project (`pytest`, package test script, focused smoke command, `rg` source assertion, or another project-native verification) and save a short report under the active work-tracking reports folder. Log that verification evidence through MCP `aegis.log` with `event_class=verification` or `plan_step=auto` so the current plan and tracker move toward closeout.
12. Run strict Aegis verification and closeout. Call MCP `aegis.verify` with `acknowledge_report_write=true` and `strict=true`, then log `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json` as strict verification evidence through MCP `aegis.log`. Call MCP `aegis.closeout_ready` before final closeout and prove it is read-only by snapshotting relevant target files before and after; the dry-run result must report `read_only=true` at the MCP wrapper level and must not write `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json` or update current-work closeout state. Then call MCP `aegis.closeout` with `acknowledge_report_write=true` and `update_handoff=true` and capture `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json` with `status=passed`.
13. Assert all workflow surfaces reference changed-source evidence. The changed source path and verification evidence must appear where closeout requires them: `sessions/current`, current plan, active `TRACKER.md`, `IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and closeout evidence matrix. Scope evidence should appear in `FINDINGS.md` and `DECISIONS.md` when appropriate. Confirm tracker checkboxes for `plan-step-scope`, `plan-step-implement`, and `plan-step-verify` are completed, pending tracking is empty, strict verification passed, and closeout summary has zero failed required checks.
14. Add focused automated coverage only for reusable mechanics uncovered by the live proof. Prefer extending existing suites rather than adding broad one-off tests. Candidate additions: a test in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py` that asserts `aegis.closeout_ready` is read-only by snapshotting target state; a docs assertion in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py` that release docs include the copied-existing-project global MCP proof and publication boundary; a registration test in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py` if the live proof reveals command rendering or verification gaps; and installer tests in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py` if closeout evidence or pending tracking needs a code fix.
15. Update release documentation and packaged mirrors. `docs/aegis/distribution.md` and `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md` should include exact local artifact/global MCP registration commands from a non-repo project, the control-plane/native-tool split, and uninstall/rollback basics. `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md` should mark the copied existing-project/global MCP row with the Task 123 evidence path and state whether it passed. `docs/aegis/release-verification-matrix.md` should reference the new evidence and clarify that policy-only claims are not enough. `docs/aegis/release-policy.md` should state the TestPyPI/PyPI decision boundary: publish only after local artifact, global MCP startup, copied existing-project live acceptance, checksum/provenance/signing decision, and documented rollback/uninstall evidence are complete. Mirror changed docs into `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/` so packaged docs stay in sync.
16. Document limitations, rollback, uninstall, and publication decision. Include how to remove the native MCP registration (`claude mcp remove aegis` or equivalent where supported, and `codex mcp remove/get` guidance as appropriate), how to remove or archive the copied target, how to clear project-local `.aegis/uv-cache` and `.aegis/uv-tools`, and how rollback remains Git-first for project changes. Explicitly list deferred items such as hosted MCP service, real PyPI install validation, signing/attestation if not produced, non-Claude adapters if not part of the live proof, and any manual client limitations encountered.
17. Preserve evidence in a durable Task 123 report, for example `release-candidate-global-mcp-proof.md` plus JSON snippets or raw transcripts. The report must include build commands, artifact paths/checksums, registration payloads, MCP `--describe-config`, discovery output, install/kickoff/log/verify/closeout outputs, closeout_ready read-only snapshot evidence, project source diff, project verification command output, no-checkout-leakage scan, docs updated, tests run, limitations, rollback/uninstall notes, and final recommendation: publish-ready, publish-deferred, or publish-blocked.
18. Keep source/package parity. Any code, docs, hook, template, or workflow changes made to source files must be mirrored into packaged assets and covered by existing package-data/release tests. Do not remove existing `scripts/codex-task aegis ...` compatibility, existing CLI command names, existing MCP tool names, or existing environment-gated local wheel smoke tests.
tasks.testStrategy:
