Verification must combine automated regression coverage with a real live acceptance run in `/tmp`.

Required live acceptance evidence:
1. Build artifacts from `/home/loucmane/codex` and capture wheel/sdist paths, SHA-256 checksums, artifact member inspection, package version, and certification report.
2. Copy the user-supplied existing project into `/tmp` and prove all mutations occur only in the copy. Capture initial snapshot and git status.
3. From the copied project directory, generate and, when available, execute native MCP registration for Aegis using `--source-mode wheel --artifact <local-wheel>` through the intended global command path. Capture `aegis mcp generate-registration`, `execute-registration`, and `verify-registration` outputs, or a structured `missing_client` result if the real native client is unavailable.
4. Start `aegis-mcp-server` from the copied project via generated `uvx --from <wheel> ... --transport stdio`, with `AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT` unset and no source-checkout `PYTHONPATH`. Assert `--describe-config` reports `asset_origin=package` and no `/home/loucmane/codex` runtime dependency.
5. Through MCP, verify discovery of all expected tools/resources/prompts, then run inspect/status/next/plan_install/install/kickoff/log/verify/closeout_ready/closeout. Assert read-only operations preserve target snapshots where applicable.
6. Confirm `aegis.install` refuses without `apply=true`, `aegis.kickoff` refuses without `apply=true`, `aegis.log` refuses without `apply=true`, and `aegis.verify`/`aegis.closeout` require acknowledgement where the MCP contract says they write reports.
7. Confirm installed workflow files exist after install/kickoff: `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/contract.md`, `.aegis/bin/aegis`, `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-plan.json`, `.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/kickoff-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, hook scripts, `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, and active work-tracking surfaces.
8. Simulate or perform the Claude-style control-plane/tool split: Aegis MCP handles workflow operations, native tools edit the copied project's source and run project verification. Confirm PostToolUse pending tracking appears after the source edit, a second mutation and Stop are blocked until S:W:H:E logging, and MCP `aegis.log` clears the pending event.
9. Assert changed-source evidence appears in session, plan, tracker, implementation, changelog, handoff, strict verification, and closeout evidence matrix. Confirm `plan-step-scope`, `plan-step-implement`, and `plan-step-verify` are completed and pending tracking is empty.
10. Snapshot before and after MCP `aegis.closeout_ready` and assert it remains read-only: no closeout report is written, current-work closeout state is not updated, and the MCP wrapper reports `read_only=true`. Then run MCP `aegis.closeout` and assert `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json` has `status=passed`, strict verification is passed, and `summary.failed_required == 0`.
11. Run a leakage scan across copied target runtime state and generated workflow surfaces, for example `rg -n '/home/loucmane/codex|AEGIS_SOURCE_ROOT|PYTHONPATH=.*codex|test.pypi.org|pypi.org|twine upload' <copy>`. Treat any runtime-state source-checkout or publication hit as a blocker unless it appears only in the preserved Task 123 evidence transcript.

Required automated tests:
- Run the focused Aegis regression slice: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_contract_docs.py`.
- Run or preserve evidence for the opt-in artifact/MCP smokes when the environment supports them: `AEGIS_RUN_WHEEL_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py::test_local_wheel_cli_smoke_when_enabled`, `AEGIS_RUN_WHEEL_MCP_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py::test_local_wheel_mcp_stdio_smoke_when_enabled`, `AEGIS_RUN_WHEEL_MCP_TARGET_SMOKE=1 python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py::test_local_wheel_mcp_real_target_project_smoke_when_enabled`, and the release certification smoke if available.
- Add targeted tests only for any behavior changed or newly formalized by this task, especially `closeout_ready` read-only snapshots, docs/package mirror assertions, and registration command edge cases.

Repository gates before completion:
- `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`
- `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`
- `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`
- `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`
- `git diff --check`

Acceptance is met only when the copied existing-project live test passes end to end, Aegis MCP starts from a non-repo location through the intended global/local-artifact command path, workflow state is created and closed out through Aegis MCP, source edits are done with native tools, `closeout_ready` is proven read-only, all workflow surfaces cite changed-source evidence, relevant automated tests pass, docs explain exact install/use/uninstall/rollback commands, and the TestPyPI/PyPI publication decision is explicitly publish-ready or deferred with concrete risks.

tasks.title: Live Aegis Acceptance Validation on Copied hpfetcher
tasks.description: Run a fresh real Claude-client acceptance validation of the wheel-backed Aegis MCP workflow against the copied hpfetcher project at /tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher, using a real BrandMark accessibility edit as the implementation payload. Capture the final Claude report, prove the original /home/loucmane/dev/hpfetcher tree was not mutated, and close the task either with passing evidence or with concrete fixes and follow-up gaps.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis summary: this repository is the Python package `aegis-foundation` from `pyproject.toml`, exposing console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`. Core deterministic workflow behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` and is mirrored into `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; CLI wiring lives in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`; native MCP registration helpers live in `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py`; MCP tools/resources/prompts are registered in `aegis_mcp/server.py`, including `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.next`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.verify`, `aegis.closeout_ready`, and `aegis.closeout`; live acceptance policy is documented in `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md`; distribution and client setup are documented in `docs/aegis/distribution.md` and `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`. Task 123 already proved the copied-existing-project/global MCP path on hpfetcher and produced evidence under `docs/ai/work-tracking/archive/20260525-task123-aegis-release-candidate-global-mcp-proof-COMPLETED/`, including the prior BrandMark accessibility edit and the CLAUDE.md merge fix. This task is the next acceptance gate: a fresh user-facing Claude client must run the workflow end to end in the current copied target `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher`, not in the original `/home/loucmane/dev/hpfetcher` tree.

Implementation approach:
1. Create normal Task 124 work-tracking/session/plan scaffolding in this repository before starting the live proof. Use a slug such as `aegis-live-hpfetcher-acceptance-validation` and create an active work-tracking folder like `docs/ai/work-tracking/active/<date>-task124-aegis-live-hpfetcher-acceptance-validation-ACTIVE/` with `TRACKER.md`, `FINDINGS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `reports/`, and `designs/`. Log the plan, the target copy path, the original source path, the intended no-mutation boundary, the Aegis artifact/registration inputs, and the live Claude test prompt before handing off to the fresh Claude client.
2. Record baseline evidence for both hpfetcher paths. From `/home/loucmane/dev/hpfetcher`, capture `git status --short`, current branch, HEAD commit, and a timestamped file snapshot sufficient to prove it remains untouched. From `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher`, capture the same baseline plus whether `.aegis`, `.claude`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `sessions`, `plans`, and `docs/ai/work-tracking` already exist. Treat any mutation under `/home/loucmane/dev/hpfetcher` as a release-blocking failure.
3. Confirm the Aegis release-candidate install path to be used by the fresh Claude client. Prefer the registered wheel-backed MCP path from Task 123: generated registration should ultimately invoke `uvx --from <local-wheel> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio`, and `aegis-mcp-server --describe-config` from the copied hpfetcher directory must report `distribution_name: aegis-foundation`, `asset_origin: package`, and `default_target_dir` resolving to `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher`, with no runtime dependency on `/home/loucmane/codex` unless explicitly recorded as a development-mode fallback.
4. Prepare a concise fresh-Claude prompt/instruction file under the Task 124 reports folder. The prompt must tell the user to start a fresh Claude client in `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher`, use the registered Aegis MCP tools for Aegis control-plane operations only, use native Claude tools for source inspection/editing/test commands, install Aegis through MCP if needed, run `aegis.next` guidance, kickoff a task for the BrandMark accessibility edit, log scope evidence, edit only the copied project, run project verification, log pending and verification evidence, run strict verification, run `aegis.closeout_ready`, run final `aegis.closeout`, and return the final Claude report/transcript.
5. The live implementation payload should be a real BrandMark accessibility edit in the copied hpfetcher project. The prior Task 123 proof identified `app/src/components/BrandMark.tsx`; this task should either apply a fresh small accessibility improvement if the copied target has reverted state, or validate/adjust the existing accessible markup if the copy already includes prior changes. Keep the edit narrow, reversible, and source-only. Do not directly edit `.aegis/`, `.claude/`, generated workflow files, `AGENTS.md`, or `CLAUDE.md` except through Aegis install/kickoff/log/verify/closeout tools.
6. Require the fresh Claude run to exercise the complete Aegis workflow, not just CLI smoke tests: `aegis.inspect`, `aegis.status`, `aegis.next`, `aegis.plan_install`, `aegis.install` with apply semantics, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log` for scope, native source edit, pending S:W:H:E logging with `pending_event_id=current` and `plan_step=auto`, native hpfetcher verification, verification evidence logging, `aegis.verify` with strict mode and acknowledgement, strict verification evidence logging, `aegis.closeout_ready`, and final `aegis.closeout` with acknowledgement and handoff update.
7. Collect and preserve the final Claude report in the Task 124 reports folder. The report should include the actual MCP tool calls or summaries, changed file paths, source diff, verification command output, Aegis report paths, final closeout status, any failures/repair loops, and a clear statement from the fresh Claude run about whether it completed without manual workflow-file edits.
8. After receiving the Claude report, validate the target state independently. Inspect `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher/.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `kickoff-report.json`, `verification-report.json`, and `closeout-report.json`; inspect `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, and the active hpfetcher work-tracking folder; confirm pending tracking is empty; confirm `plan-step-scope`, `plan-step-implement`, and `plan-step-verify` are completed; confirm changed-source evidence appears in session, plan, tracker, implementation, changelog, handoff, verification, and closeout evidence surfaces.
9. Verify original-project isolation after the live run. Re-run the baseline commands against `/home/loucmane/dev/hpfetcher` and compare status/branch/HEAD and relevant file snapshots with the pre-run evidence. Document that only `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher` was mutated. If the original project changed, stop and document the failure before attempting remediation.
10. Identify workflow gaps from the live run. Classify each issue as documentation-only, prompt/guidance issue, MCP contract issue, installer/runtime issue, hook/tracking issue, package asset parity issue, or hpfetcher-specific project issue. If the gap is in Aegis and small enough to fix safely, implement the fix in the owning source files, mirror package assets where required, and add focused regression coverage in the existing suites such as `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`, `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py`, or `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`. If the gap is not safe to fix immediately, document it as an actionable follow-up with reproduction steps, owner file/module, expected behavior, actual behavior, and blocking severity.
11. Update release evidence and docs when the outcome is known. If the live validation passes, add a durable proof report such as `live-hpfetcher-claude-acceptance.md` under the Task 124 reports folder and update `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md` plus the packaged mirror `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md` if the matrix needs a new row/status note for the copied hpfetcher fresh-Claude validation. If the validation reveals Aegis fixes, update the relevant docs only after the code/test evidence is in place.
12. Close with one of two explicit final outcomes: passing proof with evidence, including target path, report path, closeout report status, verification command, source diff summary, and original-project non-mutation proof; or actionable follow-up fixes, including exact failures, reproduction path, affected files/modules, tests added or still needed, and whether the release/publication decision remains blocked.
tasks.testStrategy:
Live acceptance verification:
1. Confirm Task 124 scaffolding exists in this repo: active work-tracking folder, session/plan references, tracker entries, findings/decisions/implementation/changelog/handoff surfaces, and a report folder containing the fresh-Claude prompt and baseline evidence.
2. Before the live run, capture `/home/loucmane/dev/hpfetcher` baseline evidence: `git status --short`, branch, HEAD commit, and relevant file checksums or snapshot manifest. Capture the same baseline for `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher`.
3. In a fresh Claude client launched from `/tmp/aegis-user-live-hpfetcher-ofLJEZ/hpfetcher`, verify the registered wheel-backed Aegis MCP is available and starts from package assets. Required evidence includes registration or discovery output, `aegis-mcp-server --describe-config` equivalent showing `asset_origin=package`, and discovery/use of the expected `aegis.*` tool surface.
4. Through MCP, run or capture the full workflow: inspect/status/next, plan_install, install with apply, kickoff with apply, scope log with apply, pending-event log with `pending_event_id=current` and `plan_step=auto`, strict verify with acknowledgement, strict verification log, closeout_ready, and final closeout with acknowledgement and handoff update.
5. Through native Claude tools, make the BrandMark accessibility edit only in the copied project, then run the project-native verification command. For hpfetcher, prefer the focused command from prior evidence when applicable: `pnpm --dir app exec biome check src/components/BrandMark.tsx`; if the project has changed, choose the closest project-native BrandMark check and record why.
6. Assert generated target artifacts exist and are internally consistent: `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json` empty or no pending events, `.aegis/reports/install-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/kickoff-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`, `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json`, `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, and the active hpfetcher work-tracking folder.
7. Assert workflow evidence surfaces cite the changed BrandMark source and verification output: session, plan, tracker, implementation, changelog, handoff, strict verification report, and closeout evidence matrix. Confirm `plan-step-scope`, `plan-step-implement`, and `plan-step-verify` completed mechanically rather than by direct manual workflow-file edits.
8. Prove `aegis.closeout_ready` remains read-only by comparing target snapshots before and after that call: it must not write a closeout report or update current-work closeout state, while the MCP wrapper reports read-only behavior.
9. After the live run, rerun the original-project baseline checks in `/home/loucmane/dev/hpfetcher` and compare them with the pre-run evidence. Acceptance requires no mutation of the original tree.
10. Preserve the final Claude report/transcript and an independent validation summary under the Task 124 report folder. The validation summary must state pass/fail, changed file(s), verification command result, closeout status, original-project isolation result, and every workflow gap found.
11. If Aegis fixes are implemented, run the focused regression slice that matches the touched area. Baseline candidates are `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_contract_docs.py`, narrowed when only docs or a single module changed. Also run repository gates before closeout: `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`.
12. The task is complete only when either the live copied-hpfetcher Claude workflow passes with durable evidence and no original-project mutation, or every blocking gap is documented with a concrete reproduction, affected code/docs paths, proposed fix, and release impact.

tasks.title: Build Public Aegis Real-Project Adoption Flow
tasks.description: Create a polished public adoption flow that turns the existing low-level Aegis install, MCP registration, and kickoff mechanics into `task-master init`-style project setup plus natural-language project work. The canonical setup command must be `aegis init` run from inside the target project. `aegis setup` may exist only as an optional compatibility alias, not as the documented happy path. After `aegis init`, a fresh Claude session in that target project should be able to receive a normal request such as `Improve BrandMark accessibility` and rely on installed Aegis files/hooks to guide readiness, local task identity, S:W:H:E tracking, verification, closeout, and handoff without a large workflow checklist prompt.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis summary: this repository is the Python package `aegis-foundation` from `pyproject.toml`, with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`. The current CLI in `aegis_foundation/cli.py` exposes low-level commands such as `inspect`, `plan-install`, `install`, `status`, `next`, `kickoff`, `log`, `verify`, `closeout`, `certify-release`, and `mcp generate-registration/execute-registration/verify-registration`; it does not yet expose the public `init`, `start`, or positional `mcp register claude` flow. Shared deterministic behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` and must stay mirrored into packaged assets under `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; native MCP command rendering lives in `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py`, where `client_argv()` already renders `claude mcp add ... -- uvx --from <spec> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio` and `run_native_client()` uses an argv list with no shell; MCP tools/resources/prompts live in `aegis_mcp/server.py`, where existing mutating tools require `apply=true`; current public docs live in `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`, `docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md`, and `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md` and currently lead with the longer inspect/plan-install/install/kickoff sequence. Existing installer behavior already preserves `CLAUDE.md` by merging an Aegis-managed block via `_merge_claude_entrypoint()`, creates `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, active work-tracking files, hooks, reports, and a project-local `./.aegis/bin/aegis` shim. Existing `kickoff()` already marks `integrations.taskmaster.required=false` and `integrations.serena.required=false`, but it still requires explicit numeric `--task`, explicit `--slug`, and a git worktree. The public flow must layer over these proven internals instead of replacing them.

Implement the feature as an additive public UX layer over the existing core:
1. Define the public adoption contract and command names. Add a short design artifact, for example `docs/aegis/public-adoption-flow.md` plus the packaged mirror under `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`. The canonical happy path must be documented as package/install once, optional native MCP registration, then per-project initialization and natural work: `aegis mcp register claude --scope user` (or simply `aegis mcp register claude` with Claude user scope as the default), `cd <target-project>`, `aegis init`, and later `aegis start "Improve BrandMark accessibility"` when a local tracked task is needed. The advanced/manual equivalents remain `aegis inspect`, `aegis plan-install`, `aegis install --apply`, `aegis mcp generate-registration`, `aegis mcp execute-registration`, `aegis mcp verify-registration`, and explicit `aegis kickoff --task ... --slug ... --title ...`. `aegis setup` may be implemented as an alias to `aegis init` only if useful, but it must not be the canonical public command in docs, prompts, generated instructions, or acceptance evidence.
2. Add a high-level `aegis init` command in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, backed by shared helpers in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`. The command must run from inside any target project with `--target-dir .` as the default and should mirror `task-master init` ergonomics: conservative defaults of `profile=generic`, `primary_agent=claude`, and `agents=[claude]` unless explicitly overridden. It should run the current inspect -> plan_install -> install/apply -> verify sequence, return structured JSON, write normal reports under `.aegis/reports/`, and stop safely on conflicts/manual-review operations using the same no-partial-write behavior as `install()`. Keep a dry-run/plan-only mode available for review, but acceptance requires plain `aegis init` to fully install the runtime into a clean target project without requiring users to know `plan-install`, `install`, `--primary-agent`, `--agent`, or `--apply`. If `aegis setup` is added, route it through the same handler and mark it as an alias in help output.
3. Add a polished native MCP registration wrapper `aegis mcp register <client>`, with `aegis mcp register claude` as the required public Claude path. It should delegate to `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py`, support package/pinned/GitHub/wheel/source modes, default to package assets for public use, default Claude scope to `user`, and support explicit `--scope project` where appropriate. It should generate the same structured payload as `registration_payload()`, execute the exact argv from `client_argv()` when registering, and verify registration through the existing native inspection logic when possible. Preserve the exact argv/no-shell behavior of `execute_registration()`. It must not manually edit `.mcp.json` as the happy path; fallback snippets remain documentation-only for clients without native registration. Preserve `aegis mcp generate-registration`, `aegis mcp execute-registration`, and `aegis mcp verify-registration` as advanced/debug surfaces.
4. Add local-task start mode for projects without Taskmaster. Expose the public command as `aegis start "normal task title"`, where the quoted title is a positional argument. Add a deterministic local task allocator, for example `.aegis/state/local-tasks.json`, that generates monotonically increasing numeric local task ids, slugs from titles, and collision-safe branch names. `aegis start "Improve BrandMark accessibility"` should allocate id/slug, then reuse the existing `kickoff()` machinery to create branch/state/session/plan/work-tracking files. Also support optional advanced `aegis kickoff --local --title "Improve BrandMark accessibility"` for users who want to stay on the kickoff surface, while preserving existing explicit `aegis kickoff --task <id> --slug <slug> --title <title>` behavior. The resulting `.aegis/state/current-work.json` must keep `integrations.taskmaster.required=false` and `integrations.serena.required=false` and must work when `.taskmaster/` and `.serena/` are absent.
5. Update installed project instructions generated by `_render_claude_entrypoint()` and `_render_contract()` so normal user language is enough. The installed `CLAUDE.md` should tell a fresh Claude session to run readiness/status/next, use `aegis init` only if the project is not installed, start local work with `aegis start "<inferred user request title>"` when no current work exists, infer a short title/slug from the user's ordinary request when reasonable, log scope before edits, use native tools for implementation, consume pending tracking with `aegis log --pending-id current --plan-step auto`, run task verification, run strict Aegis verification, close out, and only then report completion. Keep this guidance concise; it should replace the need for a giant per-session checklist prompt, not become one. Update generated `.claude/settings.json` allowlists so `aegis init`, `aegis start`, existing low-level workflow commands, and project-local `./.aegis/bin/aegis ...` equivalents are available where appropriate.
6. Preserve existing project instructions. Continue using the managed `<!-- AEGIS:BEGIN claude-runtime -->` block around Aegis instructions and preserve pre-existing `CLAUDE.md` content under `## Existing Project Instructions`. Do not introduce `.bak`, `.orig`, `.backup`, or similar sidecar files. Existing non-Aegis files must be preserved byte-for-byte except for the explicit, safe `CLAUDE.md` managed-block merge.
7. Strengthen readiness and mutation enforcement for the public flow. Ensure installed `.claude/scripts/pretooluse-gate.sh`, `posttooluse-tracking.sh`, `tracking-stop-gate.sh`, and `gate_lib.py` enforce: no persistent source mutation before READY/current work; only init/start/log/verify/closeout repair commands plus low-level install/kickoff repair equivalents are allowed while blocked; a task-scoped mutation creates pending S:W:H:E tracking; a second mutation and session stop are blocked until the pending event is logged; protected paths such as `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.aegis/`, `.claude/settings.json`, hook scripts, and generated workflow files are not manually mutated by normal implementation work.
8. Update MCP prompts/tool descriptions in `aegis_mcp/server.py` only where they help the public flow. Prefer a small number of clear prompts/descriptions that steer fresh sessions toward `aegis.status`, `aegis.next`, and public `aegis init` / `aegis start` concepts, while preserving the rule that prompts are advisory and reports/tool outputs are evidence. If MCP equivalents such as `aegis.init` or `aegis.start` are added, keep mutating-tool behavior consistent with existing MCP safety by requiring explicit `apply=true`; do not add MCP source-editing tools. Aegis remains the workflow control plane and native agent tools remain the implementation path.
9. Update docs and packaged mirrors. `docs/aegis/distribution.md`, `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`, `docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md`, `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md`, and packaged copies under `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/` should show the public commands first: `aegis mcp register claude`, `aegis init`, and `aegis start "Improve BrandMark accessibility"`. Demote the long inspect/plan-install/install/kickoff/log sequence to advanced details. Include exact commands for fresh projects, existing projects with existing `CLAUDE.md`, local wheel/pre-publication use, package mode, native Claude/Codex MCP registration, local-task start without Taskmaster, optional `aegis kickoff --local --title`, and project-local shim fallback.
10. Maintain package/source parity. Any change to installer core, rendered instructions, docs, hooks, templates, command wrappers, or public command help must be reflected in packaged assets and validated by existing package-data/release tests. Do not break existing `scripts/codex-task aegis ...`, `aegis install`, `aegis kickoff`, `aegis log`, existing MCP tool names, or current release-candidate artifact workflows.
11. Treat acceptance as behavioral. The feature is not done because docs mention an easier flow; it is done only when a fresh and an existing real target can be initialized with `aegis init`, native Claude MCP registration works through `aegis mcp register claude`, local tracked work starts with `aegis start "Improve BrandMark accessibility"`, and a fresh Claude session can be given a normal short request such as `Improve BrandMark accessibility` while the installed Aegis runtime mechanically guides readiness, task identity, S:W:H:E tracking, verification, closeout, and handoff without a large workflow prompt.
tasks.testStrategy:
Add focused automated and live acceptance coverage around the new public flow:
1. CLI parser/unit tests: extend `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py` and/or `test_aegis_invocation_contract.py` to prove `aegis init`, optional alias `aegis setup` if implemented, `aegis mcp register claude`, `aegis start "Improve BrandMark accessibility"`, and optional `aegis kickoff --local --title ...` parse correctly, delegate to the existing core, return structured JSON, and preserve backward compatibility for `inspect`, `plan-install`, `install`, explicit `kickoff`, `log`, and existing MCP registration commands.
2. Fresh-project init tests: create empty, Python, web, and backend temporary git projects, run `aegis init` from inside each target, and assert `.aegis/foundation-manifest.json`, `.aegis/contract.md`, `.aegis/bin/aegis`, `.claude/settings.json`, hook scripts, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, schemas, reports, and workflow templates exist. Verify `aegis verify --target-dir .` passes and no `.bak`, `.orig`, `.backup`, or similar files are created.
3. Existing-project preservation tests: seed an existing `CLAUDE.md` and representative project files, run `aegis init`, and assert original `CLAUDE.md` content is preserved under the existing-instructions section while the Aegis managed block is inserted or updated exactly once. Assert unrelated files remain byte-for-byte unchanged and no backup/sidecar files are written.
4. Native MCP registration tests: extend `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py` to cover the polished `aegis mcp register claude` wrapper for Claude user/project scopes and Codex where supported, including package, pinned, wheel, and source modes. Assert it renders the same safe argv as `mcp_registration.client_argv()`, never uses `shell=True`, reports `missing_client` without config-file writes when the native client is absent, and verifies expected `uvx --from <spec> aegis-mcp-server --default-target-dir . --transport stdio` registration output. Preserve coverage for `generate-registration`, `execute-registration`, and `verify-registration` as advanced commands.
5. Local-task/no-Taskmaster tests: in a project with no `.taskmaster/` and no `.serena/`, run `aegis init` plus `aegis start "Improve BrandMark accessibility"`. Assert a numeric local id is allocated in `.aegis/state/local-tasks.json`, a slug is generated, the branch is created or reused as `feat/task-<id>-<slug>`, `.aegis/state/current-work.json` has optional integrations marked not required, `sessions/current` and `plans/current` are valid, and readiness returns READY. Add parallel coverage for advanced `aegis kickoff --local --title "Improve BrandMark accessibility"` if implemented.
6. Hook enforcement tests: after `aegis init` but before `aegis start`, simulate Claude PreToolUse for a source mutation and assert it is blocked by readiness. Allow only `aegis init`, `aegis start`, log/verify/closeout repair workflow commands, and documented low-level repair equivalents while blocked. After local start and scope log, simulate a native source edit, run PostToolUse, assert `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json` is created, assert a second mutation and Stop are blocked, then run `aegis log --pending-id current --plan-step auto --plan-status completed` and assert pending tracking is cleared and S:W:H:E appears in session, tracker, implementation, changelog, handoff, and plan evidence where appropriate.
7. MCP e2e tests: extend `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py` and `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py` so the public flow works through MCP as well as CLI. If adding `aegis.init` or `aegis.start` MCP tools, assert mutating tools require explicit `apply=true`, read-only tools preserve snapshots, and tool/resource/prompt discovery remains deterministic. Existing `aegis.install`, `aegis.kickoff`, `aegis.log`, `aegis.verify`, `aegis.closeout_ready`, and `aegis.closeout` tool names must remain backward compatible.
8. Package artifact tests: update `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py` so wheel/installed-package smokes include `aegis init`, `aegis start`, and `aegis mcp register claude`, prove packaged assets include updated docs and runtime files, and confirm installed-package `--describe-config` still reports `asset_origin=package` with no dependency on `/home/loucmane/codex`.
9. Live fresh/existing project acceptance: run local wheel or package-mode Aegis into at least one fresh project and one existing project using `aegis init` as the setup command. The existing-project case should include a pre-existing `CLAUDE.md`. Preserve transcripts/reports showing `aegis init`, local start or auto-start with `aegis start "Improve BrandMark accessibility"`, native source edit, pending tracking, strict verification, and closeout. Acceptance requires no Taskmaster dependency, no backup files, preserved instructions, and passing closeout.
10. No-huge-prompt live proof: launch a fresh Claude session in a target already initialized by `aegis init` and provide only a normal short request, for example exactly `Improve BrandMark accessibility` or a similarly short user task. Do not provide the long workflow checklist from Tasks 123/124. The transcript must show Claude relying on installed `CLAUDE.md`, Aegis MCP/CLI, hooks, `aegis.status`, `aegis.next`, and `aegis start` guidance to establish readiness/task identity, log scope, perform the native edit, log S:W:H:E, run verification, close out, and hand off. Store the transcript and an independent validation summary under the Task 125 work-tracking reports folder.
11. Original-project isolation, when testing copied real projects: snapshot the original real project before and after live acceptance and prove only the copied target was mutated. Record branch/status/HEAD and relevant file checksums for both paths.
12. Repository gates before completion: run the focused Aegis regression slice (`python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_contract_docs.py`), then `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`. The task is complete only when all behavioral acceptance points pass or every remaining live-flow blocker is documented with reproduction steps, affected files, and release impact.

tasks.title: Harden Aegis Acceptance Fixture Verification
tasks.description: Replace brittle Aegis acceptance fixture assertions with runtime, AST, and structured semantic checks where possible, and document the few cases where a literal implementation style is intentionally required. Add regressions proving semantically correct fixture changes pass without forcing literal substring-oriented rewrites.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis summary: the repository is the Python package `aegis-foundation` from `pyproject.toml`, with console scripts `aegis = aegis_foundation.cli:main` and `aegis-mcp-server = aegis_mcp.server:main`. Public Aegis command wiring now lives in `aegis_foundation/cli.py` for `init`, `start`, `mcp register`, and the lower-level install/kickoff/log/verify/closeout flow; deterministic workflow behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` and must stay mirrored to `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; MCP tools and resources live in `aegis_mcp/server.py`; cross-project and target fixtures live under `tests/fixtures/aegis-target-projects/` and are exercised primarily by `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py`, `test_aegis_installer.py`, `test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py`, `test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py`, and `test_aegis_release_distribution.py`. The current acceptance-style web fixture test `test_installed_web_target_real_feature_change_updates_full_workflow` in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py` verifies the feature by literal source evidence such as the text `Add to cart` and a logged `rg` command, while also asserting exact S:W:H:E substrings across workflow surfaces. Task 125 live acceptance found that this kind of source-grep expectation can make semantically correct code look wrong and can pressure agents to rewrite code into a less idiomatic shape only to satisfy fixture literals.

Implementation approach:
1. Define an acceptance-fixture verification policy. Add a small design note, likely under `tests/fixtures/aegis-target-projects/README.md` or `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md`, explaining that application/source behavior should be verified through runtime, AST, import, JSON-schema, or parsed semantic checks whenever practical. Literal substring checks are allowed only for Aegis-owned protocol contracts such as managed block markers, command names in docs/help, required report paths, schema keys, and S:W:H:E token syntax. If an application fixture genuinely requires a literal implementation style, document that style in the fixture contract and explain why a semantic check is not available.
2. Introduce reusable semantic assertion helpers instead of scattering source-grep assertions through individual tests. A good location is a new `tests/meta_workflow_guard/aegis_acceptance_assertions.py` module. Include helpers to parse Aegis workflow evidence into structured records, for example `parse_swhe_entries(text)`, `parse_plan_table(text)`, `assert_workflow_evidence(current_work, evidence=..., handler=..., task_id=..., slug=..., plan_step=...)`, and `assert_closeout_evidence_matrix(...)`. These helpers should validate the meaning of session, tracker, implementation, changelog, handoff, plan, strict verification, and closeout evidence without requiring exact whole-line substrings except where the Aegis protocol defines those tokens.
3. Add source-level semantic validators for the fixture languages already present. For Python/backend fixtures, use Python `ast.parse`, targeted import/runtime checks, or direct function execution where safe; for example verify that `app/main.py` still exposes a callable health surface returning the expected shape rather than searching for one exact return literal. For web fixtures, prefer an AST or runtime DOM-style check over `rg`. If the existing dependency stack does not provide a TypeScript/TSX parser, choose the smallest explicit test-only parser/runtime approach and document it; if no parser is introduced, limit literal expectations to the fixture contract and add a negative test proving comments or dead strings do not satisfy acceptance. Keep any parser dependency deliberate and isolated to tests.
4. Refactor `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py` so application behavior checks are semantic. In `test_installed_web_target_real_feature_change_updates_full_workflow`, replace the direct `assert ... in source_text` and verification evidence that depends on `rg` with a helper that proves the resulting page behavior contains an actionable cart button or equivalent DOM state. The test should still verify that Aegis recorded the changed source path, that pending tracking was cleared, that `aegis verify --strict` passed, and that closeout evidence references implementation and verification evidence, but those checks should use parsed workflow records instead of brittle exact text lines wherever possible.
5. Add explicit regression fixtures for semantically correct but non-literal implementations. For the web target, include at least two equivalent source variants such as setting button content through a label variable, `append`, a helper function, or `appendChild` versus `append`, and prove both pass the semantic validator without requiring the exact `cartButton.textContent = ...` style. Add a negative variant where the expected text appears only in a comment or unused string and prove it fails. For the BrandMark-style accessibility case from Task 125, add a focused fixture or helper test that accepts semantically equivalent accessible markup, such as direct ARIA props or a documented prop-spread pattern, and rejects markup that only contains the expected words in dead code or comments.
6. Preserve meaningful literal checks for Aegis-owned contracts, but centralize them. Assertions for `<!-- AEGIS:BEGIN claude-runtime -->`, required CLI names such as `aegis init` and `aegis start`, required report paths like `.aegis/reports/verification-report.json`, and required workflow keys are still valid because they are public/runtime contracts. Make the tests distinguish those protocol literals from application implementation literals so future maintainers know which assertions are intentional.
7. Update docs and packaged mirrors only where the public acceptance contract changes. If `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md` or other Aegis docs describe acceptance evidence, update the packaged mirror under `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`. Do not rewrite unrelated distribution or invocation docs unless the test contract now requires a different public command or evidence standard.
8. Keep package/source parity intact. If any installer-rendered guidance, packaged docs, fixture expectations, or runtime assets change, mirror the change to the packaged asset tree and ensure release distribution tests still prove the wheel/package does not depend on `/home/loucmane/codex` at runtime.
9. Treat this as a test-quality and release-confidence task, not an Aegis UX redesign. Do not change `aegis init`, `aegis start`, MCP apply semantics, local task allocation, closeout behavior, or hook enforcement unless a fixture hardening test exposes a real behavioral bug; if a bug is found, isolate the fix in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, `aegis_mcp/server.py`, or the relevant hook asset and add the smallest focused regression.
tasks.testStrategy:
Add and run focused regression coverage that proves acceptance checks are semantic and still catch real failures:
1. Unit-test the new semantic helper module directly. Feed it representative session, tracker, plan, handoff, verification, and closeout snippets and assert it returns structured records for task id, slug, handler, evidence path, plan step, plan status, and report references. Add malformed examples that are missing evidence, use the wrong task id, or omit strict verification and assert they fail with clear messages.
2. Add source semantic validator tests. For Python fixtures, include passing and failing AST/runtime examples. For web fixtures, include at least one passing implementation that does not contain the exact old literal source shape, one alternate idiomatic implementation, and one failure where the expected text appears only in a comment or unused constant. For BrandMark accessibility, prove the validator accepts semantically equivalent accessible markup and rejects purely textual or dead-code matches.
3. Refactor `test_installed_web_target_real_feature_change_updates_full_workflow` and any related acceptance tests so they call the helper assertions. The test should continue to exercise install, readiness blocking, kickoff/start, scope logging, native source edit, pending tracking, implementation logging, task verification logging, strict verify, strict verify logging, closeout, and stop gate. The source behavior assertion must no longer be satisfied by a literal `rg` hit alone.
4. Add a regression test whose source intentionally uses an idiomatic but different implementation style, then run the full Aegis workflow and assert it passes. This is the key proof that semantically correct code is not forced into less idiomatic rewrites just to satisfy literal substring tests.
5. Add a negative regression where a source file contains the old expected literal in a comment or unrelated string while the actual behavior is missing. Acceptance must fail at the semantic validator, proving the new checks are stricter than a source grep.
6. Verify that Aegis protocol literals remain protected. Keep or add tests for managed block markers, public CLI command names, required report file paths, schema-backed payload fields, and S:W:H:E token parsing so the hardening does not weaken runtime contract coverage.
7. Run the focused Aegis acceptance slice after implementation: `python -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_e2e_targets.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`. If parser/runtime helper tests are placed in a new file, include that file explicitly.
8. Run repository gates before closeout: `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`, `python3 scripts/codex-task plan sync`, `python3 scripts/codex-task work-tracking audit`, `python3 scripts/codex-guard validate --include-untracked`, and `git diff --check`.
9. Completion requires both positive and negative evidence: semantically equivalent fixture implementations pass, dead-string/comment-only implementations fail, Aegis workflow evidence remains fully validated, and any unavoidable literal implementation requirement is documented in the fixture contract.

tasks.title: Add Aegis handoff auto-repair flow
tasks.description: Improve Aegis closeout handoff repair so agents do not have to manually rewrite placeholder handoff sections after closeout_ready identifies content gaps.
tasks.details: Add a deterministic repair surface, such as aegis handoff repair or stronger closeout --update-handoff behavior, that can populate Current State, What Was Done, Implementation Evidence, Verification Evidence, Strict Verification Evidence, Current Issues, and Next Steps from current-work state, plan evidence, logged S:W:H:E events, verification report, and closeout_ready failures. Preserve strict closeout gates and do not weaken handoff requirements. Add CLI and MCP coverage, plus live-style fixture tests showing a placeholder handoff can be repaired without ad hoc manual editing.

tasks.title: Add concise Aegis closeout output mode
tasks.description: Make Aegis closeout and closeout_ready output easier for public users and agents by defaulting to concise actionable summaries while preserving full JSON output for automation.
tasks.details: Add a concise default output mode for aegis closeout_ready and aegis closeout that reports status, failed required gates, warnings, pending tracking, next repair command, and report path without dumping the entire check array. Preserve structured JSON output behind an explicit --json or equivalent flag and keep MCP responses structured. Update docs, packaged docs, CLI tests, MCP tests where relevant, and live-flow acceptance notes so agents can act on closeout failures without parsing oversized terminal output.

tasks.title: Add Aegis Doctor, Repair, and Idempotency Hardening
tasks.description: Implement a read-only diagnostic and explicit safe repair foundation for Aegis state so installs, starts, logs, verification, closeout, and interrupted workflows can be replayed or recovered safely. Document the target workflow model, add CLI/MCP doctor and repair surfaces where appropriate, and preserve Taskmaster and Serena as optional integrations.
tasks.details: Codebase analysis summary: Aegis is a Python package declared in `pyproject.toml` with console scripts `aegis` and `aegis-mcp-server`. The deterministic core lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; package CLI handlers live in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`; the repository wrapper mirrors Aegis commands in `scripts/codex-task`; MCP tools and resources are registered in `aegis_mcp/server.py`; public and packaged docs are mirrored under `docs/aegis/` and `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`; regression coverage is concentrated in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py`, `test_aegis_mcp_server.py`, `test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py`, `test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py`, and `test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`. Existing core surfaces include `inspect_project`, `status`, `next_action`, `plan_install`, `install`, `initialize_project`, `start_local_work`, `kickoff`, `log_work`, `verify`, `repair_handoff`, `closeout`, and `format_closeout_summary`. Task 127 added deterministic handoff repair through `repair_handoff` and MCP `aegis.handoff_repair`; Task 128 added concise closeout output, `--json`, completed current-work state, and post-closeout readiness acceptance. Build this task on those surfaces rather than creating a parallel implementation. First, document the target state model in a new source doc such as `docs/aegis/state-recovery-model.md` and mirror it into `aegis_foundation/assets/docs/aegis/`; update `docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md`, `docs/aegis/public-adoption-flow.md`, `docs/aegis/update-rollback.md`, and matching packaged copies. The model should define states such as `not_installed`, `invalid_manifest`, `installed_no_current_work`, `in_progress_ready`, `workflow_scaffold_incomplete`, `pending_tracking`, `strict_verification_required`, `closeout_required`, `completed_closeout`, and `stale_or_broken_pointers`; it should define which commands are read-only, which commands write evidence reports, which repairs require explicit apply confirmation, and how replay behaves for `install`, `init`, `start`, `kickoff`, `log`, `verify`, `closeout_ready`, and `closeout`. Second, add a read-only core diagnostic function in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, for example `doctor(target_dir, source_root, include_repair_plan=True)`, that reuses existing helpers such as `_read_json`, `_validate_with_schema`, `_managed_assets`, `_strict_managed_files_check`, `_strict_current_work_checks`, `_strict_pending_tracking_check`, `_strict_integration_checks`, `_closeout_passed`, and `next_action`. The doctor report should be structured and deterministic: `schema_version`, `target_root`, `read_only`, `checked_at`, `status`, `summary`, `current_state`, `checks`, `repair_plan`, and `next_action`. It should check manifest presence/schema/version, managed-file presence, local CLI shim existence/executable bit, workflow template presence, current-work JSON shape and status, `sessions/current` and `plans/current` symlink health, referenced session/plan/work-tracking/report paths, work-tracking surfaces, pending tracking queue state, latest verification and closeout reports, and optional integration state. It must treat absent `.taskmaster` and `.serena` as passing unless `current_work.integrations.<name>.required` is true. Third, add a safe repair core function, for example `repair(target_dir, source_root, apply=False, scopes=None)`, that consumes the doctor plan and applies only deterministic low-risk actions. Safe actions should include recreating missing `sessions/current` and `plans/current` symlinks from `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, recreating expected empty directories such as the active reports directory, restoring missing Aegis-managed runtime files from packaged/source assets only when the path is absent and the manifest identifies the file as managed, fixing executable bits for managed scripts, and normalizing completed closeout metadata only when both current work and `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json` already prove a passed closeout. Repair must not overwrite divergent user files, must not delete or archive stale active folders by default, must not clear non-empty pending tracking, and must return actionable guidance for unsafe or manual-review cases. Mutating repair should write a repair report such as `.aegis/reports/repair-report.json`; dry-run repair should be read-only and include `would_apply` actions. Fourth, expose command surfaces. Add package CLI handlers and parser entries in `aegis_foundation/cli.py` for `aegis doctor --target-dir .` and `aegis repair --target-dir . --apply`, with concise default output and `--json` for full structured reports where useful. Mirror the same subcommands in `scripts/codex-task` under its `aegis` command. Add MCP tools in `aegis_mcp/server.py`, update `V1_TOOL_NAMES`, and follow existing response conventions: `aegis.doctor` is read-only, while `aegis.repair` previews by default and requires `apply=true` for mutation. Update prompts/resources only if they materially help agents choose doctor/repair after `aegis.next` or failed closeout. Fifth, harden replay semantics in the existing core functions. `install` and `initialize_project` should remain safe to repeat, preserving user files and avoiding backup sidecars. `start_local_work` and `kickoff` should detect existing in-progress current work: replay of the same task/title should return an `already_started` or equivalent no-op report with existing paths, a different in-progress task should be refused with a structured next action, and completed closeout state should allow starting new work. Partial workflow state should be recoverable by doctor/repair without silently allocating a second local task id or active folder. `log_work` should avoid duplicate S:W:H:E entries and duplicate plan evidence when a log command is replayed after its pending event was already consumed and the matching entry is already present. `verify` should remain safe to repeat and should not introduce duplicate manifest report entries or pending tracking ambiguity. `closeout` and `closeout_ready` should treat completed closeout state as terminal and replay-safe: repeated dry-run and final closeout should pass from completed state, avoid false readiness failures, and avoid unnecessary current-work or closeout-report churn unless an explicit refresh mode is added. Keep all implementation in the Aegis core and existing adapters; do not introduce a mandatory Taskmaster or Serena dependency.
tasks.testStrategy: Add focused regression coverage, preferably in a new `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_doctor_repair.py`, and extend existing parser/MCP tests. Verify parser coverage in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py` for `doctor`, `repair`, `repair --apply`, and JSON/summary output if implemented. Verify MCP schema/tool registration in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py`: `aegis.doctor` is read-only, `aegis.repair` previews without mutation, mutating repair requires `apply=true`, and both return structured payloads following existing `ok`, `tool`, `read_only`, `result`, and `error` conventions. Add read-only snapshot tests proving `doctor`, `repair` dry-run, `status`, `next`, and `closeout_ready` do not change any target files. Add an install replay test over the existing fixture shapes from `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py`: run `install --apply` or `init` twice, assert user files are preserved, no `.bak`/`.orig` sidecars appear, second plan is skips/no unsafe operations, and doctor reports healthy. Add missing symlink repair tests: install and start a temp git project, remove `sessions/current` and `plans/current`, assert doctor reports repairable pointer failures, assert repair dry-run does not mutate, assert repair apply recreates the symlinks from current-work paths, and assert a second repair apply is a no-op. Add stale active folder tests: create an extra `docs/ai/work-tracking/active/*-ACTIVE` folder not referenced by current work, assert doctor reports a warning or lifecycle note, assert closeout is not blocked solely by that stale folder, and assert repair does not delete/archive it without an explicit future lifecycle option. Add pending tracking tests: seed `.aegis/state/pending-tracking.json`, assert doctor reports `pending_tracking`, assert repair refuses to clear non-empty pending events and suggests `aegis log --pending-id current`, then log the pending event and replay the same log command to prove no duplicate S:W:H:E entries or plan evidence are created. Add start/kickoff idempotency tests: replay the same `aegis start` or core `start_local_work` while current work is in progress and assert no second local id, branch, session file, plan file, or active folder is allocated; attempt a different start while work is in progress and assert structured refusal; after final closeout, assert a new start is allowed. Add completed closeout tests using the existing full closeout setup pattern from `test_aegis_installer.py` and `test_aegis_mcp_server.py`: complete scope/implementation/verification/strict verification/handoff repair/final closeout, snapshot `.aegis/state/current-work.json` and `.aegis/reports/closeout-report.json`, replay `closeout --dry-run --update-handoff --json` and final `closeout --update-handoff`, assert both pass from completed state, assert no pending tracking is created, and assert completed current-work state is not reverted to in-progress or unnecessarily churned. Add optional integration tests: with no `.taskmaster` and no `.serena`, strict verify/doctor/closeout should pass integration checks as optional; if current work marks Taskmaster or Serena required, absence should fail with actionable doctor guidance. Run at minimum `pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_doctor_repair.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_invocation_contract.py`, and run the broader `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_cross_project_smoke.py` when command-surface or install behavior changes. Also run syntax/import validation for `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, `scripts/codex-task`, and `aegis_mcp/server.py`.

tasks.title: Aegis Normal-Language Workflow Acceptance and First-Pass Closeout Hardening
tasks.description: Prove and harden the installed Aegis workflow so a fresh Claude session can handle a normal user request without a long checklist prompt: detect/init/start Aegis as needed, create workflow scaffolding, use native source tools, log S:W:H:E evidence, verify, repair only through Aegis, close out, and run doctor.
tasks.details: Focus on realistic live acceptance rather than package publication. Create fresh and existing temp projects that use native MCP registration or the closest local equivalent, then prompt Claude in normal language such as 'Add a visible Add to cart button.' Record whether Claude infers the Aegis lifecycle from installed CLAUDE.md, .aegis/contract.md, hooks, and aegis next. Improve Aegis guidance, next-action responses, log defaults, handoff repair, closeout readiness, or installed docs until first-pass behavior is clean. Success requires no synthetic handler names, no direct edits to IMPLEMENTATION.md or CHANGELOG.md, no huge checklist prompt, and no hidden dependency on Taskmaster or Serena.

tasks.title: Validate Taskmaster-Backed Aegis Claude Workflow Acceptance
tasks.description: Prove and harden the fresh Claude MCP project workflow when Taskmaster already exists, ensuring natural-language requests use Taskmaster as the planning authority and Aegis as the execution control plane before Taskmaster completion.
tasks.details: Codebase analysis shows Aegis is packaged as `aegis-foundation` in `pyproject.toml`, with CLI entry points in `aegis_foundation/cli.py` and MCP tools in `aegis_mcp/server.py`. Core workflow behavior lives in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py` and must stay mirrored into `aegis_foundation/assets/scripts/_aegis_installer.py`; existing acceptance helpers live in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/aegis_acceptance_assertions.py`, fixture coverage in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py`, MCP contracts in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py`, and prior normal-language acceptance scope in `plans/2026-05-29-task130-aegis-normal-language-workflow-acceptance.md`. Extend this system for a Taskmaster-backed Claude MCP target: create or reuse a fresh fixture project that has `.taskmaster/` and installed Aegis, then run a live or scripted Claude acceptance path from a normal-language request. The required path is: discover Taskmaster work via `task-master next` and `task-master show <id>` or equivalent MCP calls; start Aegis with the explicit Taskmaster numeric id using `aegis.kickoff apply=true` rather than `aegis.start`; create the branch, session, plan, and work-tracking scaffold using that Taskmaster id; log scope with `aegis.log` before source edits; implement only through native agent tools; consume pending tracking and log implementation evidence through Aegis; run project-specific verification; run `aegis.verify strict=true`; repair failures only through Aegis tools such as `aegis.repair`, `aegis.handoff_repair`, `aegis.log`, and re-verification; run `aegis.closeout_ready` or dry-run closeout, then `aegis.closeout`; run `aegis.doctor`; only after Aegis closeout and doctor pass, mark the Taskmaster task done with `task-master set-status --id=<id> --status=done` and targeted generated-file refresh. Harden guidance in `scripts/_aegis_installer.py`, `aegis_mcp/server.py`, packaged `aegis_foundation/assets/CODEX.md`, and `docs/aegis/*` so installed Claude guidance prefers Taskmaster discovery and explicit `aegis.kickoff` whenever `.taskmaster/` has an available numeric task. Preserve the local `aegis.start` path for projects without Taskmaster, but make the distinction explicit in `aegis.next`, MCP prompts such as `aegis.start_task` and `aegis.closeout_task`, and public docs. Add guardrails and assertions that reject the known failure modes: huge checklist prompts, synthetic handler labels, direct edits under `.aegis/` workflow state, hidden Serena requirements, use of local Aegis task ids when a Taskmaster task is available, Taskmaster `done` before Aegis closeout and doctor, and broad `task-master generate` when a targeted refresh is sufficient.
tasks.testStrategy: Add focused regression tests before live acceptance. In `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py`, cover `next_action` guidance for installed projects with `.taskmaster/` and no current work: it should recommend Taskmaster discovery plus explicit `aegis kickoff --task <id>`/`aegis.kickoff`, not local `aegis start`, when a numeric Taskmaster task is available. Add fixture tests in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py` or a new acceptance module that seeds `.taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json`, runs kickoff with the Taskmaster id, and asserts `.aegis/state/current-work.json`, branch name, `sessions/current`, `plans/current`, and `docs/ai/work-tracking/active/...` all use the Taskmaster id and do not create `.aegis/state/local-tasks.json` for that task. Extend `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py` to verify MCP prompt/tool guidance says to run `task-master next/show` first and use `aegis.kickoff apply=true` for explicit numeric task ids. Extend semantic acceptance assertions to verify ordering in captured transcripts or reports: Taskmaster next/show before kickoff; kickoff before edits; Aegis strict verify, closeout, and doctor before Taskmaster done; no direct `.aegis/` hand edits; no Serena requirement unless explicitly configured. Run focused tests such as `pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_server.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer_fixtures.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_acceptance_assertions.py`, then run project guard/health checks including `python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster health`. Store live acceptance evidence under a new Task 131 work-tracking report directory and update `docs/aegis/live-acceptance-matrix.md` with a Taskmaster-backed Claude MCP row.

tasks.title: Allow Read-Only Taskmaster MCP Discovery During Aegis Bootstrap
tasks.description: Harden the installed Claude/Aegis PreToolUse gate so read-only Taskmaster MCP discovery can run while readiness is BLOCKED, without opening any Taskmaster mutation path before Aegis kickoff or after closeout rules permit it.
tasks.details:
Codebase analysis: the relevant enforcement path is `.claude/scripts/pretooluse-gate.sh`, which delegates to `.claude/scripts/gate_lib.py`. MCP classification currently happens in `mcp_is_mutation()`, using generic read-only and mutation regexes plus Aegis-specific suffix handling. Existing focused coverage lives in `tests/claude_adapter/test_pretooluse_gates.py`, including blocked readiness fixtures, blocked mutating Taskmaster MCP calls, generic read-only MCP allowance, unknown MCP fail-closed behavior, and the post-closeout Taskmaster completion allowance. Documentation that must stay aligned includes `.claude/engine/runtime-contract.md`, `.claude/engine/claude-readiness.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/aegis/invocation-contract.md`, and `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md`.

Implement a narrow, explicit Taskmaster MCP read-only allowlist in `.claude/scripts/gate_lib.py` rather than relying only on broad verb regexes. Add a helper such as `mcp_is_read_only_taskmaster_discovery(payload)` that normalizes Claude MCP tool names with the existing lower/underscore pattern and returns true only for Taskmaster discovery tools like `mcp__taskmaster_ai__help`, `mcp__taskmaster_ai__get_tasks`, `mcp__taskmaster_ai__next_task`, and `mcp__taskmaster_ai__get_task` (include the hyphenated server spelling if current settings expose `mcp__taskmaster-ai__*`). Treat these tools as non-mutating even when `.aegis/state/current-work.json` is absent, Aegis is not installed, or readiness is BLOCKED because no branch/session/plan/work-tracking state exists.

Keep fail-closed mutation behavior intact. Explicitly confirm that Taskmaster MCP tools including `set_task_status`, `update_task`, `update_subtask`, `add_task`, `expand_task`, `parse_prd`, `generate`, dependency changes, moves, and any unknown Taskmaster MCP action remain mutations when readiness is BLOCKED. Preserve the existing `payload_is_post_closeout_taskmaster_completion()` behavior: only the matching task `set_task_status done/completed` and generated-file refresh allowance after completed Aegis closeout should bypass blocked readiness, and no broader pre-kickoff Taskmaster mutation carve-out should be introduced.

Best-practice constraints for this safety gate: use a positive allowlist for side-effect-free discovery, normalize tool names once to avoid alias drift, keep unknown MCP calls persistent by default, avoid inspecting untrusted arguments for read-only tools except for path-protection checks already present, and make tests assert both the allowed path and adjacent denied paths so later broad regex changes cannot silently widen permissions.

Update docs to describe the carve-out in the same language as the existing workflow: before `aegis.kickoff`, agents may use read-only Taskmaster MCP discovery (`help`, `get_tasks`, `next_task`, `get_task`) or the CLI equivalents to find the external numeric task when available; Taskmaster mutations remain blocked until readiness is READY, except for the existing post-closeout completion bookkeeping path. Also update packaged or asset mirrors if this repo requires runtime files/docs to be mirrored into `aegis_foundation/assets/...` for distribution tests.
tasks.testStrategy:
Add focused regressions in `tests/claude_adapter/test_pretooluse_gates.py` using the existing blocked repo fixture. Assert `mcp__taskmaster_ai__help`, `mcp__taskmaster_ai__get_tasks`, `mcp__taskmaster_ai__next_task`, and `mcp__taskmaster_ai__get_task` return 0 with empty stderr while readiness is BLOCKED and no current Aegis work exists. Include the currently configured hyphenated server alias if applicable.

Add parameterized negative tests proving `mcp__taskmaster_ai__set_task_status`, `update_task`, `update_subtask`, `add_task`, `expand_task`, `parse_prd`, and `generate` still return 2 with the readiness BLOCKED message before kickoff. Keep or extend the existing unknown MCP test so unknown Taskmaster MCP names also fail closed.

Re-run the existing post-closeout tests to prove the completed-closeout allowance still permits only matching Taskmaster completion and generate refresh, while mismatched status changes and source mutations stay blocked. Run at minimum `pytest tests/claude_adapter/test_pretooluse_gates.py`; if docs/assets mirrors are updated, also run the relevant Aegis contract/doc tests such as `pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_mcp_contract_docs.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_installer.py -q` or the narrow targets that validate packaged documentation consistency.

tasks.title: Run Codex Live Aegis Acceptance Test
tasks.description: Prove and harden the Codex client path for Aegis in a safe isolated fixture, comparing behavior with the native repo and Claude MCP acceptance.
tasks.details: Create a /tmp Taskmaster-backed shop-webapp fixture with failing verification and no installed Aegis. Register or expose the local Aegis MCP/CLI path to Codex. Run a real Codex client session from normal language to observe whether it uses Aegis inspect/init/next, respects bootstrap/reload barriers where applicable, starts/kickoffs with the Taskmaster numeric task, logs scope before source edits, uses native source tools, runs project verification, strict Aegis verification, closeout, doctor, and only then marks Taskmaster done. Record exact transcript/evidence, identify parity gaps against Claude and this repo's native Codex workflow, and implement focused hardening if gaps are in Aegis guidance/runtime rather than client limitations. Keep all product testing in /tmp and do not touch real HPFetcher.

tasks.title: Private GitHub Distribution and Cross-Machine Install Flow
tasks.description: Make the private GitHub repository install path first-class for Aegis so Claude and Codex can use the workflow across machines without PyPI or public package publication.
tasks.details: Implement and document a private GitHub based install and MCP registration path for Aegis. Keep PyPI and TestPyPI out of scope. Cover install from the private repository into a fresh tmp target and into a copied real project, preserving existing CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md content. Provide exact commands for a new machine to install or register Aegis for Claude and Codex from the private repository source. Verify that MCP registration, aegis init, start or kickoff, log, strict verify, closeout, and doctor work from the private install source. Preserve current package and local source flows. Store all acceptance evidence under the task work-tracking reports folder, including transcripts or command logs for Claude and Codex where feasible. Do not mutate the real HPFetcher project directly; use tmp copies for real-project acceptance.

tasks.title: Add Isolated Native MCP Registration Smoke Command for Aegis
tasks.description: Add an Aegis smoke-test command that exercises native Claude and Codex MCP registration in temporary client homes/config directories, verifies the resulting registration, and emits structured evidence without mutating real user configuration.
tasks.details:
Implement this as an additive smoke surface on top of the existing native MCP registration helpers in `aegis_foundation/mcp_registration.py`, package CLI wiring in `aegis_foundation/cli.py`, and the repo wrapper in `scripts/codex-task`. Add a command such as `aegis mcp smoke-registration` and mirror it under `python3 scripts/codex-task aegis mcp smoke-registration`.

Core behavior:
- Accept `--client` as repeatable or `all` with supported clients `claude` and `codex`; accept the same source-selection inputs already used by `RegistrationRequest`: `--source-mode`, `--package-spec`, `--package-version`, `--github-url`, `--github-ref`, `--artifact`, `--target-dir`, `--transport`, `--uv-cache-dir`, and `--uv-tool-dir`.
- Create a temporary smoke root with isolated homes/config dirs per client. For Claude, isolate `HOME`, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, and any Claude-specific config path the native CLI honors. For Codex, pre-create `CODEX_HOME` inside the smoke root before invoking `codex mcp add`, and ensure `HOME`/`XDG_CONFIG_HOME` also point at temporary locations.
- Do not write `.mcp.json`, Codex config files, `~/.claude`, `~/.codex`, or the caller's real `CODEX_HOME`. The command must fail tests if native clients observe real user home/config paths.
- Reuse `mcp_registration.client_argv()`, `execute_registration()`, `inspect_argv()`, and `verify_registration()` where practical, but extend the execution path to accept an environment override so smoke runs can supply isolated `env` to `subprocess.run` without shell execution.
- Run registration followed by verification for each requested client against the supplied source mode/ref. Verification should assert the same required fields as `verify_registration_output()`: server name `aegis`, `uvx`, expected `source_spec`, `aegis-mcp-server`, `--default-target-dir`, `--transport`, and registered UV env values.
- Treat missing native clients as a clean `skipped`/`missing_client` result for that client, not an unhandled exception. Include the attempted argv, rendered command, isolated paths, and reason in the JSON payload.
- Emit one structured JSON document to stdout with aggregate status (`passed`, `failed`, `skipped`, or `partial`), per-client results, source metadata, temp path metadata, and safety notes such as `isolated-client-home`, `real-user-config-not-touched`, and `codex-home-precreated` for Codex.
- Add optional `--report-file` for JSON evidence and optional `--runbook-file` or `--markdown-report-file` for a concise Markdown report, following existing `scripts/codex-task` report-file patterns. Ensure `--dry-run` or `--keep-temp` semantics are explicit if added; by default temp dirs should be cleaned after the run while still reporting their logical layout.
- Update `docs/aegis/mcp-client-setup.md` and the release verification matrix to document the smoke command as the isolated way to prove native registration without touching user config.

Implementation should preserve current native registration commands (`register`, `generate-registration`, `execute-registration`, `verify-registration`) and add the smoke workflow without changing their existing output contracts.
tasks.testStrategy:
Add focused pytest coverage in `tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py` and release/documentation assertions where appropriate.

Required tests:
- Unit-test the smoke helper with fake `claude` and `codex` executables on `PATH`, similar to `_write_fake_native_client()`, proving registration and verification are invoked with exact argv and no shell.
- For Codex, assert the command pre-creates temporary `CODEX_HOME`, passes it to the subprocess environment, and never uses an existing real `CODEX_HOME` from the parent environment.
- For Claude, assert `HOME` and config-related env values point under the smoke temp root and that no repo or user config path is written.
- Test package, pinned, private-github with `--github-ref`, and source checkout modes by validating the expected `uvx --from` source spec appears in verification results.
- Test missing clients by monkeypatching `shutil.which`/`PATH` so one or both clients are absent; the command should return structured skipped/missing-client evidence cleanly and should not attempt fallback config-file writes.
- Test JSON stdout is parseable and stable, `--report-file` writes the same structured payload, and optional Markdown output summarizes aggregate and per-client outcomes.
- Add CLI tests for both `aegis mcp smoke-registration` and `python3 scripts/codex-task aegis mcp smoke-registration` to ensure package CLI and repo wrapper stay aligned.
- Run targeted tests: `python3 -m pytest tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_native_mcp_registration.py tests/meta_workflow_guard/test_aegis_release_distribution.py`.

tasks.title: Document agent-runtime-first workflow design and seed hardening backlog
tasks.description: Import the corrected agent-runtime-first Aegis workflow design into this repo and seed the first implementation backlog tasks.
tasks.details: Acceptance: docs/aegis/agent-runtime-first-workflow-design.md exists as the authoritative corrected design note; docs/aegis/hpfetcher-workflow-system-design.md is retained as superseded historical input; Taskmaster contains the first five agent-runtime hardening implementation tasks. Test strategy: run Taskmaster health and guard validation after task generation.

tasks.title: Block unclassifiable mutating payloads
tasks.description: Close the fail-open path where a non-empty hook payload that cannot be parsed/classified is allowed to proceed.
tasks.details: Agent-runtime-first rationale: an agent must not be allowed to mutate a project when Aegis cannot render a verdict on the action. Acceptance: non-empty malformed/unknown PreToolUse payloads for mutating-capable tools fail closed; empty payloads or known read-only payloads keep existing behavior; structured denial includes a remediation message and does not crash the hook; tests cover malformed JSON, missing tool fields, unknown MCP mutation payloads, and known safe read-only cases.

tasks.title: Short-circuit read-only commands before readiness
tasks.description: Classify read-only inspection and test commands before running full readiness, allowing harmless observation and verification without task-state blocking.
tasks.details: Agent-runtime-first rationale: blocking inspection makes agents blind and increases bad mutation risk; read-only commands do not need task attribution. Acceptance: known read-only Bash commands such as ls, cat, sed, rg, git diff/status/show, task-master next/show/list, and non-writing test commands bypass readiness; commands with redirects, writes, generated outputs, status changes, or unknown shell features remain mutation-gated; tests cover classifier positives/negatives and prove readiness is not invoked for read-only payloads.

tasks.title: Protect workflow-owned surfaces from direct agent edits
tasks.description: Prevent agents from forging Aegis readiness or evidence by directly editing workflow-owned state, while preserving sanctioned Aegis CLI/MCP mutation paths.
tasks.details: Agent-runtime-first rationale: sessions, plans, work-tracking, and .aegis state are authority surfaces; direct Edit/Write can forge in-progress state. Acceptance: direct agent Edit/Write/Bash mutations to .aegis state, sessions, plans, and active work-tracking are blocked unless routed through sanctioned Aegis handlers; sanctioned commands including aegis kickoff, aegis log, handoff repair, closeout, and plan sync continue to work and write structured evidence; tests prove direct edits are denied and real Aegis-owned flows do not self-deadlock.

tasks.title: Add degraded-event breadcrumb and closeout enforcement
tasks.description: Introduce a narrow audited degraded mode for gate infrastructure failure on non-destructive actions, with closeout and doctor visibility.
tasks.details: Agent-runtime-first rationale: agents need a sanctioned recovery path when Aegis infrastructure itself fails, but unsafe actions must still fail closed. Acceptance: gate infrastructure crashes on non-destructive actions write tamper-evident degraded event evidence and allow only the permitted action class; protected/destructive/workflow-state actions still fail closed; doctor reports degraded events; closeout refuses completion until degraded events are acknowledged or resolved; tests cover breadcrumb schema, closeout failure, doctor reporting, and mandatory fail-closed classes.

tasks.title: Add read-only Aegis reconciliation report
tasks.description: Add a read-only reconcile command that reports drift between Taskmaster status, branch/PR/git merge truth, active Aegis work, and local stubs without auto-mutating status.
tasks.details: Agent-runtime-first rationale: final done truth should converge on merge/git reality, but first the system must prove it can detect drift safely. Acceptance: command reports merged-but-not-done, done-but-not-merged, abandoned in-progress branch, stale local/ad hoc stubs, and multi-PR epic ambiguity; gh is optional acceleration and git-only fallback works; command is read-only, emits JSON and human summaries, and has tests for branch ancestry, missing remotes, merged PR metadata, and ambiguous/multi-PR cases.
